Ayahs

طسٓ ۚ تِلْكَ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱلْقُرْءَانِ وَكِتَابٍ مُّبِينٍ (۱)
Ta, Seen. These are the signs of the Quran and a manifest Book, (1)
هُدًى وَبُشْرَىٰ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۲)
a guidance and good news for the faithful (2)
ٱلَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَهُم بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ (۳)
—those who maintain the prayer and pay the zakat, and who are certain of the Hereafter. (3)

Key Points

Zakat has two meanings: one is the specific meaning, which is the well-known Zakat (obligatory charity), and the other is the general meaning, which is helping the needy in any way. In this Ayah, the second meaning of Zakat is intended, because this Surah was revealed in Mecca, while the command for formal Zakat was issued in Medina. «یؤتون الزّكاة»

The word «مُبین» (Mubin), derived from ibānah, is used both in the sense of "clear" (which is technically an intransitive verb/adjective) and in the sense of "clarifying" or "making clear" (which is a transitive verb/adjective).

Messages

1- One of Allah's traditions (Sunan) is to make the Revelation, despite its superiority and high status, accessible to humanity. «تلك»

2- The scope of guidance through speaking and listening is wider than through writing and reading. (The word Qur'an precedes Kitab (Book)). «القرآن و كتاب»

3- The movement of the Prophets is cultural and involves recitation and writing. «آیات القرآن و كتاب»

4- The Qur'an is a truly great Book. (The indefinite form «كتابٍ», without the definite article alif and lam and accompanied by tanwin, is a sign of its greatness).

5- The Qur'an is comprehensible to people. «كتاب مبین»

6- Guidance and propagation must be accompanied by clear content and a direct and decisive method. «تلك - مبین»

7- Guidance has degrees and stages: Initial guidance: «هُدىً للنّاس» (Guidance for mankind); Complementary guidance: «هُدىً... للمؤمنین» (Guidance... for the believers).

8- Among the distinctions of the Qur'an are that it is written: «كتاب» (Book); that it is clear and clarifying: «مبین» (Mubin); that it guides: «هُدىً» (Guidance); and that it gives glad tidings: «بُشرى» (Glad Tidings).

9- The primary mission of the Qur'an is guidance and glad tidings; its attention to other sciences and issues is secondary. «هُدىً و بُشرى»

10- Faith is the key to guidance and salvation. «هُدىً و بشرى للمؤمنین»

11- Receptivity is the main condition for being guided. «للمؤمنین»

12- To promote goodness, we should honor the good people. «الّذین...»

13- In honoring people, the focus is on their deeds, not their names or titles. «یقیمون... یؤتون...»

14- Action is a sign of faith. «للمؤمنین الّذین یقیمون...»

15- Even believers who perform prayer and Zakat need Divine guidance for the continuation of their work. «هُدىً... للمؤمنین الّذین...» (Guidance... for the believers who...). On the Day of Resurrection, too, man will be in need of a stage of guidance. «یهدیهم ربّهم بایمانهم»(1)

16- A prayer is valuable only if it is performed with the conditions of validity and perfection. «یقیمون» (The meaning of establishing prayer is performing it with its conditions).

17- The relationship with Allah precedes the relationship with people. «یقیمون الصلاة ویؤتون الزكاة» (Prayer comes before Zakat).

18- Since prayer and the relationship with God pave the way for other good deeds, its name is mentioned first. «الصلاة»

19- Continuity of action gives value to the action. «یقیمون - یؤتون» (The present tense verb indicates continuity and permanence).

20- One of the distinctions of Islam is that it places Divine and human issues, spiritual and material matters, and worldly and otherworldly concerns side by side. «الصلاة - الزكاة»

21- In propagation, first mention the deeds that do not cost the people (financially). (First prayer, then Zakat). «الصلاة - الزكاة»

22- The believer feels responsible regarding social issues and the needs of the deprived and the hungry. «یؤتون الزكاة»

23- Wherever sensitivity is required, repeat the phrase. (Prayer and Zakat are introduced with a single word «الّذین» (those who), but certainty in the Hereafter is emphasized by using the word «هُم» (they) twice.)

24- Prayer and Zakat are valuable only if they are accompanied by faith in the Hereafter. «و هُم بالاخرة»

25- The people of certainty are a special group. «هم بالاخرة هم یوقنون» (They are certain of the Hereafter). The repetition of the word «هُم» indicates that this group is a select group.

26- Believers are the winners; they build themselves through prayer, solve societal problems through Zakat, and secure their future through certainty in the Hereafter. «یقیمون الصلاة... الزكاة... یوقنون»

27- Knowledge can turn into forgetfulness, but certainty is accompanied by constant attention. «یوقنون»

28- Conjecture and mere knowing are not enough; heartfelt belief and certainty are necessary. «یوقنون» (1) (Yunus, 9).

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah An-Naml This Surah has ninety-three Ayahs, and its well-known name is "Naml" (The Ant), due to Ayah 18, which concerns the story of the ants and Prophet Sulayman (Solomon), عليه السلام. However, the names "Sulayman" and "Tasin" have also been given to it. This entire Surah was revealed in Mecca—during various events and occasions—and «بسم اللّه الرّحمن الرّحیم» (In the Name of Allah, the Most Compassionate, the Most Merciful) appears in it twice: once at the beginning of the Surah, and again in Ayah 30, at the beginning of the letter from Prophet Sulayman to the Queen of Sheba. The struggles of four great Divine Prophets (Musa, Sulayman, Salih, and Lut, علیهم السلام) against the deviant nations of their time are mentioned in this Surah. The most detailed account is the encounter of Prophet Sulayman with the Queen of Sheba and how she came to believe in Allah. The speech of birds like the Hoopoe and insects like the ant, the presence of individuals from the Jinn in Sulayman’s army and court, and finally, the bringing of Bilqis’s throne from Yemen to Sham (Syria/Levant) in the blink of an eye, constitute another part of the Ayahs of this Surah.

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ زَيَّنَّا لَهُمْ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ فَهُمْ يَعْمَهُونَ (٤)
As for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have made their deeds seem decorous to them, and so they are bewildered. (4)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَهُمْ سُوٓءُ ٱلْعَذَابِ وَهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمُ ٱلْأَخْسَرُونَ (٥)
They are the ones for whom there is a terrible punishment, and they are the ones who will be the biggest losers in the Hereafter. (5)

Key Points

In this Ayah and Ayah 108 of Surah Al-An'am, considering evil deeds as good is attributed to Allah. While the Noble Qur'an, in several other instances, considers this act to be the work of Satan. This is because God has established the system of existence based on causes and means, and the actions that occur can be attributed to any one of these causes. For example, one can say: the key opened the door, and one can say: my hand opened the door, and also one can say: I myself opened the door, because the key is in my hand and the hand is under my control. Here too, Satan makes the evil deed appear beautiful, but man becomes accustomed to it through the repetition of the evil deed, and becoming accustomed to an action through repetition is a Divine tradition (Sunnah).

Characteristics of the Losers

Losers are of several types: A: Khāsir (loser), B: la-fī khusr (in loss), C: Khusrān Mubīn (manifest loss), D: Akhsarūn (the greatest losers).

A: One whose life has been ruined. «قل اِنّ الخاسرین الّذین خَسروا انفسهم»(2) B: One who is not among the people of faith and righteous deeds. «اِنّ الانسان لفى خُسر الاّ الّذین آمنوا»(3) C: One who worships God with instability. «اِنْ أصابه خیرٌ اطمأنّ به و اِن أصابته فتنة انقلب على وَجهِه خَسِرالدّنیا و الآخرة ذلك هو الخُسرانُ المبین»(4) D: Those who are misguided, yet think they are following the right path. «أَلّذین ضَلّ سَعیهم فى الحیاة الدنیاو هم یحسبون اَنّهم یحسنون صُنعا»(5)

  • We read in narrations: The Akhsarūn (greatest losers) are those who do not pay Zakat, insist on sin, have the power to speak the truth but do not, and are the most oppressive individuals. They fix their worldly affairs by ruining their religion.

2) Zumar, 15. 3) Al-Asr, 2. 4) Al-Hajj, 11. 5) Al-Kahf, 104.

Messages

1- Lack of faith in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the ground for the manifestation of evil deeds as attractive. (The faithless person considers nudity to be civilization, luxury to be personality, fraud to be cleverness, lying and hypocrisy to be signs of politics, and colonialism to be a sign of power.) «اِنّ الّذین لایؤمنون... زیّنّا»

2- The misunderstanding and confusion of faithless individuals is the greatest punishment and scourge from Allah. «لایؤمنون... فهم یعمهون»

3- If we believe in the Day of Judgment, we will repent from many deeds that appear beautiful to us. «لایؤمنون - زیّنّا»

4- The one who does not consider the Day of Judgment to be His bright future, the changing world confuses and transforms him every moment. «لایؤمنون - یَعمهون»

5- Disbelief in the Day of Judgment is the cause of the greatest losses. «الّذین لایؤمنون بالاخرة... هم الاخسرون»

Ayah

وَإِنَّكَ لَتُلَقَّى ٱلْقُرْءَانَ مِن لَّدُنْ حَكِيمٍ عَلِيمٍ (٦)
Indeed you receive the Quran from One who is all-wise, all-knowing. (6)

Messages

1- The recipient of the revelation is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله. «و انّك لتلقّى القرآن»

2- The Quran is the manifestation of Divine knowledge and wisdom. «القرآن من لدن حكیم علیم»

3- The Prophet's knowledge is Divinely bestowed (ladunni). «من لدن»

4- In the descent and reception of the Quran, there is no untrustworthy intermediary. «لتلقّى القرآن من لدن حكیم علیم»

5- The ordinances of religion originate from the source of Divine knowledge, and wisdom is hidden within every command. «حكیم علیم»

Ayahs

إِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِأَهْلِهِۦٓ إِنِّىٓ ءَانَسْتُ نَارًا سَـَٔاتِيكُم مِّنْهَا بِخَبَرٍ أَوْ ءَاتِيكُم بِشِهَابٍ قَبَسٍ لَّعَلَّكُمْ تَصْطَلُونَ (۷)
When Moses said to his family, ‘Indeed I descry a fire! I will bring you some news from it, or bring you a firebrand so that you may warm yourselves.’ (7)
فَلَمَّا جَآءَهَا نُودِىَ أَنۢ بُورِكَ مَن فِى ٱلنَّارِ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهَا وَسُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۸)
When he came to it, he was called: ‘Blessed is He who is in the fire and who is [as well] around it, and immaculate is Allah, the Lord of all the worlds!’ (8)
يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَنَا ٱللَّهُ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۹)
‘O Moses! Indeed I am Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise.’ (9)

Key Points

The name of Prophet Moses (Musa) is mentioned 136 times in the Quran, his story is recounted in 34 Surahs, and approximately 900 verses concern the events of the Children of Israel (Bani Israel). Prophet Moses was traveling from Madyan towards Egypt with his pregnant wife. Night, cold, and the desert storm on one hand, and his wife's labor on the other, compelled Moses to seek help; the verses under discussion refer to this event. Among all possibilities, it seems that the intent of «مَن فى النّار» (who is in the fire) is Prophet Moses, who was in the vicinity of the fire, and the intent of «مَن حَولها» (who is around it) is his family or the prophets who were in the Holy Land (al-Ardh al-Muqaddasah).

Fire in the Quran: * Iblis boasted about it. «خلقتنى من نار» (6) * The disbeliever is punished by it. «تصلى ناراً حامیه» (7) * Abraham remained safe in it. «یا نار كونى برداً» (8) * Moses went toward it, but became a prophet. «انست ناراً ... نودى» (9) * Dhul-Qarnayn used it to melt metals and build a barrier. «قال انفخوا حتّى اذا جعله ناراً» (10)

6) Al-A'raf, 12. 7) Al-Ghashiyah, 4. 8) Al-Anbiya, 69. 9) An-Naml, 7 - 8. 10) Al-Kahf, 96.

Messages

  1. The story of Moses is worthy of mention and a source of remembrance, and should not be forgotten. «اذ قال...»

  2. Sometimes, special favors are found while seeking help for one's wife and family. «آتیكم بشهاب... نُودِى»

  3. The man is responsible for managing the affairs of his wife's life. «آتیكم بشهاب قبس لعلّكم تصطلون»

  4. Prophets lived ordinary lives. «آتیكم بشهاب قبس لعلّكم تصطلون»

  5. We are commanded by the apparent (outward actions); hidden favors are the work of Allah. «فلما جاءها نودى»

  6. Allah's speech to Moses, the reception of the message of prophethood, the display of miracles, and the commissioning of other prophets in that region are the secret to the blessing. «بورك مَن فى النار و مَن حولها»

  7. We should not consider the Divine calls to the prophets as illusion or fantasy. (Next to the word «نودى» (He was called), «سبحان اللّه» (Glory be to Allah) is mentioned, meaning Allah is far removed from the possibility that this call was an illusion, fantasy, or whisper.)

  8. The commissioning of prophets is among the affairs of Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). «نودى - رب العالمین»

  9. The requirement of Lordship is Might (Azzah) and Wisdom (Hikmah). «ربّ العالمین ... العزیز الحكیم»

Ayahs

وَأَلْقِ عَصَاكَ ۚ فَلَمَّا رَءَاهَا تَهْتَزُّ كَأَنَّهَا جَآنٌّ وَلَّىٰ مُدْبِرًا وَلَمْ يُعَقِّبْ ۚ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ لَا تَخَفْ إِنِّى لَا يَخَافُ لَدَىَّ ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ (۱٠)
‘Throw down your staff!’ When he saw it wriggling, as if it were a snake, he turned his back [to flee], without looking back. ‘O Moses! ‘Do not be afraid. Indeed the apostles are not afraid before Me, (10)
إِلَّا مَن ظَلَمَ ثُمَّ بَدَّلَ حُسْنًۢا بَعْدَ سُوٓءٍ فَإِنِّى غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۱)
nor those who do wrong and then make up for [their] fault with goodness, for indeed I am all-forgiving, all-merciful.’ (11)

Key Points

The word "جانٌ" (jānn) either means a small snake, which is the form the staff initially took, or a large snake that moved with the speed of a small snake. Regarding where the phrase "الاّ مَن ظلم" (except him who has wronged) relates, there are several possibilities. The best of them is that a phrase is implied in the Ayah, meaning that non-prophets experience fear, except for those who have committed a wrong and then performed a good deed, for they too have no fear due to Allah being Forgiving (Ghafūr) and Merciful (Rahīm). Fear is of two types: instinctive (gharzī) and spiritual (ma'nawī). The phrase "لا تَخَف" (fear not) concerns instinctive fear, while spiritual fear, which is the fear of the Divine Presence, is addressed in other verses.

Messages

1- A heavenly call must be accompanied by a sign to be effective. «نودى... الق عصاك»

2- The instrument of the miracle is an ordinary tool. «عصاك»

3- Before guiding others, one must possess sufficient knowledge, experience, and maturity. (Here, Moses sees the miracle so that he may speak powerfully in Pharaoh's palace). «رَآها»

4- Prophets are under the tutelage of their Lord, and Allah commands and forbids them. «لا تخف»

5- Whoever knows Allah is present and sees himself in His presence does not fear anyone other than Allah. «لا یخاف لدى المرسلون»

6- Al-Khā'in Khā'if (The betrayer is fearful). Whoever commits wrong must fear, unless he compensates for his sin, because Allah is Forgiving. «لا یخاف... الاّ مَن ظَلَم»

7- Transforming a sin into a good deed is different from erasing a sin, pardoning a sin, or performing a good deed after a bad one; for example, placing the means of sin in the hands of the righteous. «بَدّلَ حُسنا»

8- Allah's forgiveness follows man's good deed and compensation for the sin. «بَدّل حُسنا... فانّى غفور رحیم»

Ayahs

وَأَدْخِلْ يَدَكَ فِى جَيْبِكَ تَخْرُجْ بَيْضَآءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوٓءٍ ۖ فِى تِسْعِ ءَايَـٰتٍ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَقَوْمِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا فَـٰسِقِينَ (۱۲)
‘Insert your hand into your shirt. It will emerge white and bright, without any fault—among nine signs meant for Pharaoh and his people. Indeed they are a transgressing lot.’ (12)
فَلَمَّا جَآءَتْهُمْ ءَايَـٰتُنَا مُبْصِرَةً قَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱۳)
But when Our signs came to them, as eye-openers, they said, ‘This is plain magic.’ (13)

Key Points

The intent behind the nine miracles (معجزات نه گانه) is the miracles that Moses عليه السلام brought forth before Pharaoh (فرعون), otherwise, Prophet Moses had more miracles. Furthermore, every miracle can be counted as two miracles, because the staff turning into a serpent is one miracle, and the serpent returning to a staff is another miracle, but the Quran has considered only one side of it as a miracle. However, the nine miracles of Moses consist of: 1. "Yad al-Bayda" (the shining of the hand). 2. The staff turning into a serpent. 3. The devastating flood (Tufan) for the enemies. 4. "Jarad" (the dominance of locusts over crops and trees). 5. "Qummal" (a type of plant pest that destroyed grains). 6. "Dafadi'" (the invasion of frogs from the Nile River into people's lives). 7. "Dam" (the general affliction of nosebleeds; or the water of the Nile River turning the color of blood).(11) 8. Famine and drought.(12) 9. The splitting of the sea.(13)

  • Other miracles of Moses عليه السلام include: the gushing forth of twelve springs from the rock (14), the descent of Mann and Salwa (15) (a special and delicious sap from trees—like manna—and special birds, similar to pigeons).

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of "min ghayri sū’" (من غیر سوء) is that this whiteness of the hand was not due to the disease of leprosy or vitiligo.(16)

11) Al-A'raf, 133. 12) Al-A'raf, 130. 13) Al-Baqarah, 50. 14) Al-Baqarah, 60. 15) Al-Baqarah, 57. 16) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Wrath and mercy, fear and hope, are necessary side by side. (The luminous hand alongside the serpent) "jānn... bayḍā’"
  2. For some, a single argument, reminder, or miracle is not enough. "tis'a āyāt"
  3. On the one hand, the disbelievers requested miracles, and on the other hand, they called the miracles sorcery and magic. "qālū hādhā siḥrun mubīn"

Ayah

وَجَحَدُوا۟ بِهَا وَٱسْتَيْقَنَتْهَآ أَنفُسُهُمْ ظُلْمًا وَعُلُوًّا ۚ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (۱٤)
They impugned them, wrongfully and out of arrogance, though they were convinced in their hearts [of their veracity]. So observe how the fate of the agents of corruption was! (14)

Key Points

Abu 'Umar al-Zubayri asked Imam Sadiq عليه السلام about the types of disbelief (kufr) mentioned in the Quran. The Imam said: Disbelief is of five types. One of its types is that a person has belief and recognition in his heart, yet he still denies it. Then he recited this Ayah: «وجحدوا بها واستیقنتها انفسهم»(17)

17) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 389.

Messages

1- Denial is the ground for corruption. «جحدوا ... مفسدین»

2- Knowledge and certainty are ineffective if not accompanied by piety (taqwa). «واستیقنتها - ظلماً و علوّاً»

3- Many disbelievers have faith in their hearts, and the motive for their denial is not ignorance, but rather oppression (zulm) and seeking superiority. «ظلماً و علوّاً»

4- Make history and its study a means of drawing lessons (cibra). «فانظر»

5- The ultimate end of the corruptors is downfall and ruin. «كیف كان عاقبة المفسدین»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا دَاوُۥدَ وَسُلَيْمَـٰنَ عِلْمًا ۖ وَقَالَا ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى فَضَّلَنَا عَلَىٰ كَثِيرٍ مِّنْ عِبَادِهِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱٥)
Certainly, We gave knowledge to David and Solomon, and they said, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, who granted us an advantage over many of His faithful servants.’ (15)

Key Points

It is possible that the knowledge given to Prophets David (Dawud) and Solomon (Sulayman) was the knowledge of judgment, due to the Ayah: «و آتیناه الحكمة و فصل الخطاب»(18), meaning, We bestowed upon David wisdom and decisive judgment. And also due to the Ayah: «كلاّ آتینا حكماً و علما»(19). And perhaps the intended knowledge is the knowledge of conversing with birds, due to the Ayah: «علّمنا منطق الطّیر»(20). And perhaps it is the knowledge of armor making: «و علّمناه صَنعة لبوس»(21). However, it is better to interpret the knowledge generally, meaning the knowledge of administering the state.

Question: Why does Allah bestow special blessings upon some of His servants? Is this compatible with justice?

Answer: Firstly, the meaning of justice is not that we must give everyone equally. Is a teacher who gives different grades to different students, or a doctor who prescribes different medicine for different patients, unjust? Secondly, special blessings also entail special responsibilities. Thirdly, we have no claim upon God (Allah) such that He must grant us whatever we desire. Fourthly, divine favors are based on wisdom and the conditions that the individual or society creates. As the poet says:

Since we were such, we were that way; Since we became this, we became this way.

Individuals, through sincerity, effort, knowledge, prudence, economy, justice, and unity of word, create conditions within themselves that become the basis for receiving divine favors and special blessings.

Of course, sometimes special favors are due to the reward for deeds performed by a person's parents, and Allah grants the reward of their work to their descendants. Just as in the story of Moses (Musa) and Khidr, Allah commanded the two Prophets to repair a wall beneath which was a treasure belonging to two orphan children, so that they might use that treasure in the future, because the children's parents were righteous and virtuous. «وكان ابوهما صالحا»(22)

Special Divine Teachings

Allah has given specific knowledge to specific individuals and mentioned them in the Quran, including:

  1. Adam, the knowledge of all things. «و علّم آدم الاسماء كلّها»(23)
  2. Khidr, esoteric knowledge and interpretation (so that Moses might become his student). «هل اتّبعك على ان تعلّمَن...»(24)
  3. Joseph (Yusuf), the knowledge of dream interpretation. «علّمنى ربّى»(25)
  4. David (Dawud), the knowledge of armor making. «و علّمناه صَنعة لَبوس»(26)
  5. Solomon (Sulayman), the knowledge of the language of birds. «علّمنا منطق الطیر»(27)
  6. Solomon's assistant, the knowledge by which he brought the royal throne from one country to another. «قال الّذى عنده علم من الكتاب»(28)
  7. Talut, military sciences. «و زاده بسطة فى العلم والجسم»(29)
  8. The Noble Messenger (Rasul Akram) and other Prophets, knowledge of the unseen (Ghayb). «فلایظهر على غیبه احدا الاّ مَنِ ارتضى من رسول»(30)

Messages

  1. The knowledge of the Prophets is ladunni (divinely bestowed) and was granted to them through divine inspiration. «آتینا»
  2. We must preserve the respect and status of individuals. (The father's name is mentioned first, then the son's name). «داود و سلیمان»
  3. Among the divine blessings, knowledge holds a distinct status. «آتینا... علماً»
  4. Knowledge is valuable only when it is placed in the hands of righteous individuals. «آتینا داود وسلیمان علماً»
  5. The best phrase for thanking Allah is «الحمدللّه». «الحمدللّه»
  6. Knowledge is one of the criteria for superiority. «آتینا... علماً... فضّلنا»
  7. Some of Allah's servants are superior even to David and Solomon. «فضّلنا على كثیر»
  8. In no position should we consider ourselves superior to everyone else. «على كثیر»

Ayah

وَوَرِثَ سُلَيْمَـٰنُ دَاوُۥدَ ۖ وَقَالَ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ عُلِّمْنَا مَنطِقَ ٱلطَّيْرِ وَأُوتِينَا مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ ۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَهُوَ ٱلْفَضْلُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۱٦)
Solomon inherited from David, and he said, ‘O people! We have been taught the speech of the birds, and we have been given out of everything. Indeed this is a manifest advantage.’ (16)

Key Points

The meaning of inheritance (irath) in this Ayah is the inheriting of wealth and governance, not knowledge and prophethood. This is because prophethood is not transferable, and the knowledge of the prophets, being non-acquisitional, is also not inheritable. Therefore, the tradition (hadith) which states that prophets do not leave an inheritance—and based on which Fadak was taken from Fatimah al-Zahra علیها السلام—is inconsistent with this Ayah and must be rejected. (Fatimah al-Zahra علیها السلام recited this very Ayah in defense of Her right to the first Caliph.(31) And in Tafsir Namuneh, it is narrated from Sirat al-Halabi that Abu Bakr was affected by the words of Fatimah al-Zahra علیها السلام, returned the deed of Fadak, and wept, but Umar took the deed back and tore it up!) Imam Ali علیه السلام said: "I know, by the command of Allah, the language of the birds and all creatures on land and sea."(32)

31) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 32) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq.

Messages

1- Inheritance has a very long history. «و ورث سلیمان ...»

2- The knowledge of the prophets is Divine. «عُلّمنا»

3- Express the Divine blessings. «علّمنا منطق الطیر» (We were taught the language of the birds). In another place, He says: "And as for the favor of your Lord, report [it]." «و امّا بنعمة ربّك فحدّث»(33)

4- Birds possess consciousness and the power of speech. «منطق الطیر»

5- Knowledge precedes all other blessings. First «علّمنا» (We were taught), then «اوتینا من كلّ شى ء» (We were given of everything).

6- We must consider our knowledge and resources as coming from Allah, not from ourselves. «علّمنا - اوتینا»

7- Faith and reliance (tawakkul) are not contradictory to possessing resources. «و اوتینا من كل شى ء»

8- We must consider blessings as coming from His grace, not from our own merit. «انّ هذا لهوالفضل المبین» 33) Ad-Duha, 11.

Ayah

وَحُشِرَ لِسُلَيْمَـٰنَ جُنُودُهُۥ مِنَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ وَٱلطَّيْرِ فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ (۱۷)
[Once] Solomon’s hosts were marched out for him, comprising jinn, humans and birds, and they were held in check. (17)

Key Points

The word yūza'ūn is derived from the root waz' (on the measure of jam') meaning to restrain or hold back. Whenever this term is used regarding an army, it means that, in order to prevent the dispersal of forces, the front of the army is held back until the rest of the individuals join the ranks. (34) Īzā' means the wise distribution of forces such that each one is placed in its proper position and aligned in a single line. (35)

The jinn and the birds mentioned in this Ayah are given as examples; otherwise, the wind, the clouds, and other things were also under the command of Sulayman (Solomon). The Noble Qur'an has spoken about the jinn many times, and one of its Surahs is named after them. Here, we point out a few of their characteristics:

  • The jinn are conscious beings and are addressed by the Lord. «يَا مَعْشَرَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنسِ» (36)
  • Some of them are believers and some are disbelievers. «وَإِنَّا مِنَّا الْمُسْلِمُونَ» (37), «وَإِنَّا مِنَّا الصَّالِحُونَ وَمِنَّا دُونَ ذَلِكَ» (38)
  • They have males and females, they reproduce, and naturally, they have desires. «لَمْ يَطْمِثْهُنَّ إِنسٌ قَبْلَهُمْ وَلَا جَانٌّ» (39)
  • They performed diving and construction work for Sulayman. (40) «وَالشَّيَاطِينَ كُلَّ بَنَّاءٍ وَغَوَّاصٍ» (41)

34) Tafsir Namūneh. 35) Al-Tahqīq fī Kalimāt al-Qur’ān. 36) Al-An'am, 130. 37) Al-Jinn, 14. 38) Al-Jinn, 11. 39) Al-Rahman, 56. 40) For further explanation regarding the jinn, refer to Surah Al-Jinn and Qamus al-Qur'an, Vol. 2, pp. 61-73. 41) Sad, 37.

Messages

1- Sulayman reviewed his armies. «وَحُشِرَ لِسُلَيْمَانَ جُنُودُهُ»

2- Man possesses the power to bring the jinn and the birds under his command (by the permission of Allah). «وَحُشِرَ لِسُلَيْمَانَ جُنُودُهُ مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنسِ وَالطَّيْرِ»

3- Some Prophets held governance. «حُشِرَ لِسُلَيْمَانَ جُنُودُهُ»

4- The jinn can assist humans. «مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنسِ»

5- The authority (Wilayah) of the Prophets is not limited only to humans. «مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنسِ وَالطَّيْرِ»

6- Order is valuable everywhere, but in an army and military force, it is a necessity and a special virtue. «فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ»

7- Training, instruction, and readiness are necessities for the Divine army. «وَحُشِرَ لِسُلَيْمَانَ... يُوزَعُونَ»

8- The knowledge and wisdom of Sulayman became the means and prerequisite for achieving all those capabilities and the subjugation of forces. «وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا دَاوُودَ وَسُلَيْمَانَ... أُوتِينَا مِن كُلِّ شَيْءٍ... وَحُشِرَ لِسُلَيْمَانَ...»

Ayah

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَتَوْا۟ عَلَىٰ وَادِ ٱلنَّمْلِ قَالَتْ نَمْلَةٌ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّمْلُ ٱدْخُلُوا۟ مَسَـٰكِنَكُمْ لَا يَحْطِمَنَّكُمْ سُلَيْمَـٰنُ وَجُنُودُهُۥ وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۱۸)
When they came to the Valley of Ants, an ant said, ‘O ants! Enter your dwellings, lest Solomon and his hosts should trample on you while they are unaware.’ (18)

Key Points

Naml linguistically means extensive movement with small feet. (42) The word namlatun (نَملةٌ), due to its tanwin (nunation), can be interpreted as a large, commanding ant, in which case, management and command exist in the life of the ants. Alternatively, the tanwin can signify that the ant is unknown (unidentified), in which case, the message of the Ayah is that even if an unknown person gives a sincere warning, we should pay attention to their caution.

42) Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Quran.

Messages

1- Animals, using their own consciousness, choose a region for life. «وادالّنمل»

2- Conversation, commanding, and forbidding also exist among animals. «قالت نملة یا ایّها النمل»

3- Among ants, there exists command, information dissemination, and obedience. «قالت نملة ... اُدخلوا»

4- Animals possess consciousness, recognize harms and dangers, and warn one another. «اُدخلوا مساكنكم»

5- The instinct to avert potential harm exists even in animals. «اُدخلوا... لایحطمنّكم»

6- One who does not warn his fellow beings upon sensing danger is less than an ant. «لا یحطمنّكم»

7- We should be careful when walking so as not to trample the ants. «لا یحطمنّكم»

8- Prophets lived like others in their ordinary affairs. «لا یحطمنّكم سلیمان»

9- The corruption of the leader is the prelude to the corruption of the people. «لا یحطمنّكم سلیمان و جنوده»

10- The ant recognizes human individuals, and even their occupations. «سلیمان و جنوده»

11- The friends of Allah (Awliya Allah) do not intentionally or knowingly harm even an ant. «و هم لا یشعرون» The ants are aware of the justice and piety of Prophet Sulayman (عليه السلام) and know that that great personality would not knowingly oppress even an ant.

Ayah

فَتَبَسَّمَ ضَاحِكًا مِّن قَوْلِهَا وَقَالَ رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِىٓ أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَىَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَٰلِدَىَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَـٰلِحًا تَرْضَىٰهُ وَأَدْخِلْنِى بِرَحْمَتِكَ فِى عِبَادِكَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۱۹)
Whereat he smiled, amused at its words, and he said, ‘My Lord! Inspire me to give thanks for Your blessing with which You have blessed me and my parents, and that I may do righteous deeds which please You, and admit me, by Your mercy, among Your righteous servants.’ (19)

Key Points

In Ayah 16, it was mentioned that Prophet Solomon (Sulayman) عليه السلام said: "We know the language of the birds." From this Ayah, it becomes clear that knowing the language of the birds was merely an example, because the Prophet also knew the speech and conversation of the ant. The word 'Īzā‘ (ایزاع) is higher than tawfīq (divine success/enablement) and is something similar to inspiration (ilhām), similar to the Ayah: «و اوحینا الیهم فعل الخیرات» (43) meaning, We inspired the prophets to perform good deeds. (44)

A righteous deed ('amal sālih) alone is not sufficient; entering the group of the righteous (sālihīn) is important, because: * Sometimes the deed is righteous, but the individual is not righteous internally (psychologically) and becomes afflicted by hypocrisy (riyā’), seeking fame (sum‘ah), arrogance (ghurūr), self-admiration (‘ujb), and nullification (of deeds, habt). * Sometimes the deed is righteous, but a deviation occurs in the end, and the person does not achieve a good outcome (‘āqibat bi khayr). * Sometimes the deed is righteous, but it involves a kind of individualism, and the person is unwilling to accompany the servants of Allah. «اعمل صالحا ... فى عبادك الصالحین»

43) Al-Anbiyā’, 73. 44) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- One of the principles and conditions of leadership and management is broad-mindedness (sa‘at-e sadr). Prophet Solomon hears the phrase «لایشعرون» (they perceive not) from an ant, yet He does not take offense and smiles. «فتبسّم»

2- Listen to criticism and the word of truth from anyone and welcome it. (Solomon approved of the ant's speech.) «فتبسّم» Accepting criticism is a virtue.

3- Allah places His chosen friends (awliyā’) under His special training. «و هم لایشعرون - فتبسّم» Allah involves Solomon, despite His high status and dominion, with the speech of an ant, so that He may establish balance within His being.

4- We should be happy and cheerful when even ants are hopeful of our justice. «و هم لایشعرون - فتبسّم»

5- The laughter of the prophets is a smile (tabassum), not a loud laugh (qahqaha). Solomon smiled while He was amused. «فتبسّم ضاحكا»

6- Solomon understood the ant's words and paid attention to His subordinates. «من قولها»

7- Supplication (du‘ā) is not exclusive to times of distress; the prophets prayed even at the peak of their greatness and power. «ربّ»

8- We should ask Allah for the divine enablement (tawfīq) to be grateful. «ربّ اوزعنى أن اشكر»

9- Man must be grateful for the blessings he has received so far, and regarding future responsibility, he must think about righteous deeds, gaining Allah's pleasure, and joining the righteous. «اشكر - اعمل صالحا ...»

10- Children must be grateful both for the blessings Allah has bestowed upon them and for the blessings He has bestowed upon their parents. «علىّ و على والدىّ»

11- Gratitude to Allah is not only verbal; righteous deeds and the proper use of blessings are also divine gratitude. «اشكر... اعمل صالحا»

12- Although Solomon possesses vast armies, strong dominion, and knows the language of animals, He is still focused on righteous deeds, not pursuing pleasure, expansion of power, or pride. «اعمل صالحا»

13- The supplication and effort of rulers must be directed toward performing good work. «اعمل صالحا»

14- A deed is good only if it is pleasing to Allah. «صالحاً ترضاه»

15- A good deed is valuable if it earns the pleasure of Allah, not just the pleasure of people. «صالحاً ترضاه»

16- The highest goal in the governance of the righteous is the pleasure of Allah. «ترضاه»

17- Prophets also need divine assistance. «اوزعنى... ادخلنى»

18- Living in a righteous society is valuable, not living among unrighteous people merely for the sake of comfort, income, or pleasure. «ادخلنى... فى عبادك الصالحین»

19- Do not consider your current possession of resources and power as a sign of having attained divine mercy, and pray for your own growth and elevation. «و ادخلنى»

20- Man's resources are not the result of his own work but originate from divine mercy. «برحمتك»

21- Everyone, under all circumstances, must seek refuge in divine mercy, even the prophets. «برحمتك»

22- Individualism (going it alone) is forbidden. «و ادخلنى برحمتك فى عبادك الصالحین»

Ayahs

وَتَفَقَّدَ ٱلطَّيْرَ فَقَالَ مَا لِىَ لَآ أَرَى ٱلْهُدْهُدَ أَمْ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْغَآئِبِينَ (۲٠)
[One day] he reviewed the birds, and said, ‘Why do I not see the hoopoe? Or is he absent?’ (20)
لَأُعَذِّبَنَّهُۥ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا أَوْ لَأَا۟ذْبَحَنَّهُۥٓ أَوْ لَيَأْتِيَنِّى بِسُلْطَـٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ (۲۱)
‘I will punish him with a severe punishment, or I will behead him, unless he brings me a credible excuse.’ (21)
فَمَكَثَ غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ فَقَالَ أَحَطتُ بِمَا لَمْ تُحِطْ بِهِۦ وَجِئْتُكَ مِن سَبَإٍۭ بِنَبَإٍ يَقِينٍ (۲۲)
He did not stay for long [before he turned up] and said, ‘I have alighted on something which you have not alighted on, and I have brought you from Sheba a definite report. (22)
إِنِّى وَجَدتُّ ٱمْرَأَةً تَمْلِكُهُمْ وَأُوتِيَتْ مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَلَهَا عَرْشٌ عَظِيمٌ (۲۳)
I found a woman ruling over them, and she has been given everything, and she has a great throne. (23)
وَجَدتُّهَا وَقَوْمَهَا يَسْجُدُونَ لِلشَّمْسِ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ فَصَدَّهُمْ عَنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ فَهُمْ لَا يَهْتَدُونَ (۲٤)
I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allah, and Satan has made their deeds seem decorous to them—thus he has barred them from the way [of Allah], so they are not guided— (24)

Key Points

Some have said that the meaning of the hoopoe (hudhud) here is a specific hoopoe; and they consider their evidence to be the definite article (alif and lām) in "الهدهد" (Al-Hudhud) and its ability to recognize humans and schools of thought, the explanation of which we will read in the subsequent verses.(45) Abu Hanifa asked Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: Why did Prophet Sulayman (Solomon) inquire only about the hoopoe among the birds? The Imam replied: Because it sees water inside the earth, just as we see oil in a crystal vessel.(46) Some have mentioned examples of the hoopoe's punishment, including: separating it from its mate, plucking its feathers, exposing it to the sun, banishing it from Sulayman's court, or placing it in a cage with an enemy.(47)

45) Tafasir Furqan and Fi Zilal. 46) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 47) Tafasir Ruh al-Bayān and Kanz al-Daqā’iq.

Messages

  1. Inquiring about subordinates, inspecting their work, and searching for their welfare are among the Islamic, ethical, social, educational, and managerial principles. «تفقّد» (Inquiry/Inspection)
  2. Major issues should not distract you from minor issues. «تفقّد الطّیر» (Inspecting the birds)
  3. Do not judge hastily and do not accuse your subordinate forces; perhaps there is an unknown problem. «مالى لا أرى الهدهد» (Why do I not see the hoopoe?)
  4. Sharp observation, precision, and mastery are necessary conditions for leadership. «مالى لا أرى الهدهد»
  5. The ruler and judge must possess specific characteristics, including: A: Affection and precision. «تفقّد الطیر» B: Humility. «مالى لا أرى الهدهد» C: Firmness against the transgressor. «لاُعذّبنّه او لاذبحنّه» D: Giving the accused a chance. «لیأتینّى»
  6. Strict supervision over the attendance and absence of personnel is a necessity in organizational work, just as unjustified violation of regulations and abandoning one's post is an unforgivable sin. «ام كان من الغائبین»
  7. Punishing a transgressing animal is permissible. «لاُعذّبنّه»
  8. The animal understands punishment. «لاُعذّبنّه»
  9. If the absence of a bird requires such a severe threat, then what is our duty regarding our numerous absences and non-attendances? «لاُعذّبنّه»
  10. Prophets both bring the law and implement it. «لاُعذّبنّه»
  11. Multiple penalties must be established for punishing transgressors so that we do not reach a deadlock in implementing the law. «لاُعذّبنّه او لاذبحنّه»
  12. Decisiveness and acceptance of excuses must go hand in hand. «لاُعذّبنّه - لیأتینّى»
  13. In management and organization, negligence is forbidden. «لاُعذّبنّه - لاَذبحنّه - لیأتینّى» (The presence of the letter "lām" [the emphatic particle] in the three phrases is a sign of decisiveness.)
  14. To maintain order and deter transgressors, it is permissible to set a heavy penalty for a minor transgression. «عذاباً شدیدا»
  15. Do not close the path of defense to the accused. «او لیأتینّى بسلطان مبین»
  16. Animals possess a consciousness that allows them to provide reasoning and justification for their actions. «لیأتینّى بسلطان»
  17. In the presence of logic and evidence, even Sulayman submits to the hoopoe. «لیأتینّى بسلطان»
  18. In an organization, every action and mission requires a decree and authorization. «بسلطان مبین»
  19. He whose account is clean has nothing to fear from calculation. «فمكث غیربعیدفقال»
  20. An animal can attain knowledge that a human has not reached. «احطت بمالم تحط»
  21. Travel and exploration are the keys to acquiring new information. «احطّ»
  22. Freedom of speech is one of the characteristics of a righteous government. «احطت بمالم تحط به»
  23. Knowledge and wisdom are not related to age, gender, or appearance. «احطت بمالم تحط»
  24. Prophets are also under Divine training. Sulayman, who said: «اوتینا من كل شى ء» (We have been given of all things), is told by a bird: I know something that you do not know. «احطتُ بمالم تحط به»
  25. In the government of the Prophets, flattery and fear do not exist. «احطتُ بمالم تحط به»
  26. Leaders and government officials should not limit their sources of information to specific individuals or groups. (The hoopoe reports to the Sultan.) «احطت - جئتك»
  27. In reports, state confirmed and certain news. «بنبأ یقین»
  28. The existence of individuals or an organization for gathering news from outside the governmental region is necessary (not for espionage, but for invitation, guidance, or defensive readiness). «من سبأ»
  29. What is important in news is its certainty, whether the reporter is great or small, human or animal. «بنبأ یقین»
  30. Government officials should rely only on certain news. «بنبأ یقین»
  31. Some animals possess high levels of understanding. «انّى وجدتُ امراة» (Indeed, I found a woman). The hoopoe recognizes concepts such as man and woman, crown and throne, government and ownership, Monotheism and polytheism, the sun and prostration, Satan and his adornments, truth and falsehood, guidance and misguidance.
  32. Listening to the words of others is a sign of etiquette and one of the conditions of management, even if the speaker is an animal. «انّى وجدتُ امراة»
  33. A woman can rule; «تملكهم» (ruling them), but the rule of a woman over society is strange even for a bird. «وجدتُ امرأة - وجدتُها و قومها»
  34. The government of Prophet Sulayman was not initially global. «امرأة تملكهم»
  35. The formation of a system and government has historical precedence. «تملكهم»
  36. Factors of deviation include: A: A misguided leader. «وجدتها و قومها یسجدون للشمس» B: Glitter and glamour. «یسجدون للشمس» C: Resources and welfare. «اوتیت من كل شى ء» D: Satan and his adornments. «زین لهم الشیطان»
  37. Enjoyment of Divine blessings is not a sign of proximity or distance (Allah gives both to Sulayman and to a disbelieving woman. In previous verses, Sulayman said: «اوتینامن كل شى ء» (We have been given of all things); regarding Bilqis, we read: «و اوتیت من كل شى ء» (And she has been given of all things)).
  38. Allah's blessings in the hands of the righteous are a clear and manifest favor; «اوتینا من كل شى ء انّ هذا لهوالفضل المبین» (We have been given of all things; indeed, this is the manifest favor), but in the hands of others, they become a crown, a throne, and a great seat. «اُوتیت من كل شى ء و لها عرش عظیم» (She has been given of all things, and she has a great throne)
  39. Man, by nature, is a worshipper; if he does not worship the truth, he turns to falsehood. «وجدتها و قومها یسجدون...»
  40. People adopt the religion accepted by their rulers and leaders. "الناس على دین ملوكهم" (People follow the religion of their kings). «وجدتها و قومها یسجدون للشمس»
  41. Report every problem to its proper authority (The report of deviation and polytheism must be told to Sulayman). «یسجدون للشمس»
  42. Sun worship has historical precedence, both in the time of Ibrahim عليه السلام; «هذا ربّى هذا اكبر»(48) (This is my Lord; this is greater) and in the time of Sulayman عليه السلام. «یسجدون للشمس»
  43. The best means of misleading people is to make their ugly deeds seem fair-seeming. «زیّن لهم الشیطان»
  44. The bad past of individuals is not a sign of their definite deviation in the future (Bilqis and her people were deviated, but they were later guided). «لایهتدون»
  45. In reports, state the core news and leave the analysis and interpretation to the experts. «انّى وجدتُ... فهم لایهتدون» (What the hoopoe reported was correct, but where it analyzed, it spoke incorrectly, because they were later guided.) 48) Al-An‘ām, 78.

Ayahs

أَلَّا يَسْجُدُوا۟ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى يُخْرِجُ ٱلْخَبْءَ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تُخْفُونَ وَمَا تُعْلِنُونَ (۲٥)
so that they do not prostrate themselves to Allah, who brings forth the hidden in the heavens and the earth, and He knows whatever you hide and whatever you disclose. (25)
ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ رَبُّ ٱلْعَرْشِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ ۩ (۲٦)
Allah—there is no god except Him—is the Lord of the Great Throne.’ (26)

Key Points

The word "خَب ء" (khab') means covered and hidden. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: "المرءُ مَخبُوءٌ تَحتَ لِسانه" (49) The worth and personality of a person are hidden beneath his tongue. Until a man speaks, his fault and his skill remain concealed. Examples of bringing forth the hidden things in the heavens and the earth are the growth of plants and the falling of rain. The cluster is hidden inside the seed, and the Divine power brings it out from the heart of the seed. All things that move from potentiality to actuality are instances of this verse, "یخرج الخبأ" (He brings forth the hidden).

49) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 140.

Messages

1- Satan's goal in beautifying people's evils is that they should not prostrate to Allah. "زیّن لهم الشیطان - ألاّ یسجدوا"

2- Whoever does not prostrate before Allah, even animals criticize him. "ألاّ یسجدوا"

3- Prostration (Sujud) is the manifestation of monotheism (worship of Allah). "ألاّ یسجدوا للّه"

4- Natural factors are tools and means for Allah to display His power. "یخرج الخبأ" Although water, soil, and light play a role, He is the true Bringer Forth.

5- The universe is in the presence of Allah. "یعلم ما تخفون و ما تُعلنون"

The knowledge of a single atom is not hidden from Him For the concealed and the manifest are one in His sight.

6- Among the reasons for our prostration are His power, "یخرج الخبأ," His knowledge, "یعلم," His Oneness, "لا اله الاّ هو," and His Lordship and Greatness. "ربّ العرش العظیم"

Ayahs

۞ قَالَ سَنَنظُرُ أَصَدَقْتَ أَمْ كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ (۲۷)
He said, ‘We shall see whether you are truthful, or if you are one of the liars. (27)
ٱذْهَب بِّكِتَـٰبِى هَـٰذَا فَأَلْقِهْ إِلَيْهِمْ ثُمَّ تَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ فَٱنظُرْ مَاذَا يَرْجِعُونَ (۲۸)
Take this letter of mine and deliver it to them. Then draw away from them and observe what [response] they return.’ (28)

Key Points

Correspondence and communication by prophets with disbelievers (kuffar) and polytheists (mushrikin) has a long history, and it was also the practice (sirah) of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله. He sent letters to the leaders of the countries of Iran and Rome. Sending messages, cultural advisors, ambassadors, and agents, and taking the initiative in these matters for the sake of achieving guidance (irshadi) and cultural goals, is part of the practice (sirah) of the prophets.

Messages

1- Gullibility is forbidden; rejecting the words of others without reason is also forbidden. «سننظر» (Regarding important matters, do not rely on a single report.)

2- The claims and propaganda of others should not prevent us from investigating. (The hoopoe claimed: My news is certain, but Solomon said: We must investigate) «سننظر»

3- Investigation is not limited to reports from a wicked person (fasiq); rather, investigation and scrutiny are necessary even when in doubt. «سننظر»

4- Neither confirmation nor denial, but investigation. «سننظر أصَدقتَ أم كنت من الكاذبین»

5- Mention positive points before negative points. «أصَدقتَ ام ...»

6- We do not become part of the truthful (sadiqin) group with a single truth, but we become part of the liars with a single lie. («صَدقتَ» is used as a verb, but «كاذبین» is used as an active participle, especially with the word «كنتَ».)

7- Birds are also placed at the disposal of executing the commands of Allah and His vicegerents (awliya'). «اِذهب بكتابى»

8- The pen is one of the most important tools for guidance (hidayah) and instruction (irshad). «اِذهب بكتابى»

9- We should take the initiative in guiding and instructing others and establishing proper and beneficial communication with them. «اِذهب بكتابى»

10- In selecting individuals for missions, whoever possesses greater knowledge and sensitivity will have priority. «اِذهب»

11- For achieving divine goals, there is no impediment to a man writing a letter to a woman. «كتابى»

12- In matters of belief (aqidah), after hearing distressing reports, one must show an immediate reaction and religious zeal (ghayrah). «اذهب بكتابى»

13- We must be trustworthy (amin) in conveying content and concepts. «بكتابى هذا»

14- Covert and unseen reconnaissance is the deepest type of knowledge acquisition. «ثم تولّ»

15- One cannot deal simply with complex political systems and governments. «القه ثم تولّ»

16- The ability to design and plan is one of the prerequisites for management. «اِذهب بكتابى - ثمّ تولّ»

17- We should give others an opportunity to reflect. «فانظر ماذا یرجعون»

18- The condition for success in guidance (irshad) and enjoining good (amr bi al-ma'ruf) is recognizing the states and morale of others. «فانظر ماذا یرجعون»

19- Identifying the individual is not enough; the system and those involved must be taken into account. «الیهم - عنهم - یرجعون»

20- Keep the enemy's reactions under surveillance. «فانظر ماذا یرجعون»

Ayahs

قَالَتْ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمَلَؤُا۟ إِنِّىٓ أُلْقِىَ إِلَىَّ كِتَـٰبٌ كَرِيمٌ (۲۹)
She said, ‘O [members of the] elite! Indeed a noble letter has been delivered to me. (29)
إِنَّهُۥ مِن سُلَيْمَـٰنَ وَإِنَّهُۥ بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ (۳٠)
It is from Solomon, and it begins in the name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful. (30)
أَلَّا تَعْلُوا۟ عَلَىَّ وَأْتُونِى مُسْلِمِينَ (۳۱)
[It states,] ‘‘Do not defy me, and come to me in submission.’’ ’ (31)

Key Points

Regarding why Bilqis referred to Sulayman's letter as a "noble writing" (كتاب كریم), several reasons have been cited, including: A: Due to the blessed phrase "بسم اللّه الرّحمن الرّحیم". B: The letter being sealed with Sulayman's seal. C: The instructive content of the letter. D: Due to Sulayman's status; and perhaps it was due to all of these factors.

Messages

1- Bilqis had companions and advisors with whom she shared news and events. «یا ایّها الملؤا» (O chiefs/counselors)

2- Literacy for a woman is a virtue. «القى الىّ كتاب كریم»

3- Bilqis was sharp-witted and possessed high perceptive power. «كتاب كریم»

4- Let us begin guidance and propagation with kindness and mercy. «بسم اللّه الرّحمن الرّحیم»

5- Even when writing just a few words, we should write the phrase "بسم اللّه الرّحمن الرّحیم" in full, not abbreviated. «انّه بسم اللّه الرّحمن الرّحیم»

6- Seeking superiority against the Prophets is seeking superiority against Allah. «ألاّتَعلوا علىّ»

7- Avoiding arrogance and seeking superiority is the prerequisite for submitting to the truth. «ألاّ تعلوا علىّ و أتونى مسلمین»

8- Let us write letters simply and concisely. Let us omit titles and clarify objectives. Let us avoid cursing and insults. Let us present mercy and firmness side by side. «بسم اللّه... لا تعلوا... و أتونى»

Ayahs

قَالَتْ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمَلَؤُا۟ أَفْتُونِى فِىٓ أَمْرِى مَا كُنتُ قَاطِعَةً أَمْرًا حَتَّىٰ تَشْهَدُونِ (۳۲)
She said, ‘O [members of the] elite! Give me your opinion concerning my matter. I do not decide any matter until you are present.’ (32)
قَالُوا۟ نَحْنُ أُو۟لُوا۟ قُوَّةٍ وَأُو۟لُوا۟ بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ وَٱلْأَمْرُ إِلَيْكِ فَٱنظُرِى مَاذَا تَأْمُرِينَ (۳۳)
They said, ‘We are powerful and possess a great might. But it is up to you to command. So consider what orders you will give.’ (33)

Key Points

The words "fatwā" and "fatā" share the same root. "Shābb" is used for any young person, but "fatā" is used for a young person who is prudent, brave, wise, and resolute. "Fatwā" also means a wise, mature, and complete statement.

Messages

1- Bilqis valued her advisors and consulted with them. «ما كنت قاطعة امراً حتّى تشهدون»

2- Sometimes power and resources prevent the acceptance of the truth. «نحن اولوا قوّة و اولوا بأس» (We are possessors of strength and possessors of might.) (Power and position had made Bilqis's advisors arrogant.)

3- In governmental matters, one person must have the final say. «والامر الیك» (And the command is yours.) (Others offer opinions, but the right to make the decision belongs to one person). «فانظرى ماذا تأمرین» (So consider what you will command.)

4- Bilqis was the commander of the armed forces of her time. «نحن اولوا قوّة و اولوا بأس و الامر الیك»

Ayah

قَالَتْ إِنَّ ٱلْمُلُوكَ إِذَا دَخَلُوا۟ قَرْيَةً أَفْسَدُوهَا وَجَعَلُوٓا۟ أَعِزَّةَ أَهْلِهَآ أَذِلَّةً ۖ وَكَذَٰلِكَ يَفْعَلُونَ (۳٤)
She said, ‘Indeed when kings enter a town, they devastate it, and make the mightiest of its people the weakest. That is how they act. (34)

Messages

1- Confessing the characteristics and traits of kings is a sign of Balqis's courage. «قالت اِنّ الملوك...»

2- Balqis was concerned both about defeat by Sulayman and about her attachment to the prosperity of her country. «افسدوها و جعلوا اعزّة اهلها اذلّة»

3- Do not rely solely on your own power; take others into account as well. (Balqis's entourage said: «نحن اولوا قوّة و اولوا بأس شدید», but Balqis warned them not to underestimate Sulayman's power). «قالت ان الملوك...»

4- The method of non-Divine governments is to create corruption and humiliation among the region and the people. «كذلك یفعلون»

5- The affliction of governance and power is domination-seeking, warmongering, and destruction. «الملوك... افسدوها و جعلوا اعزّة اهلها اذلّة»

Ayah

وَإِنِّى مُرْسِلَةٌ إِلَيْهِم بِهَدِيَّةٍ فَنَاظِرَةٌۢ بِمَ يَرْجِعُ ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ (۳٥)
I will send them a gift, and see what the envoys bring back.’ (35)

Messages

1- Woman is not inherently warlike. «انّى مرسلة الیهم بهدیّة»

2- Movements that have a material motivation are calmed upon achieving material gains. «بهدیّة» (The characteristic of kings is their susceptibility to gifts.)

3- A gift is sometimes, in reality, a bribe and hush money. «بهدیّة»

4- Enemies also test us by sending gifts. «مُرسلة الیهم بهدیّة»

5- Tyrants (Taghuts) and kings knew that the Prophets did not have material goals, yet they still tried to attract and pacify them. «مُرسلة الیهم بهدیّة»

Ayahs

فَلَمَّا جَآءَ سُلَيْمَـٰنَ قَالَ أَتُمِدُّونَنِ بِمَالٍ فَمَآ ءَاتَىٰنِۦَ ٱللَّهُ خَيْرٌ مِّمَّآ ءَاتَىٰكُم بَلْ أَنتُم بِهَدِيَّتِكُمْ تَفْرَحُونَ (۳٦)
So when he came to Solomon, he said, ‘Are you aiding me with wealth? What Allah has given me is better than what He has given you. Indeed, you are proud of your gift! (36)
ٱرْجِعْ إِلَيْهِمْ فَلَنَأْتِيَنَّهُم بِجُنُودٍ لَّا قِبَلَ لَهُم بِهَا وَلَنُخْرِجَنَّهُم مِّنْهَآ أَذِلَّةً وَهُمْ صَـٰغِرُونَ (۳۷)
Go back to them, for we will come at them with hosts which they cannot face, and we will expel them from it, abased and degraded.’ (37)

Messages

1- The motivation of Allah's chosen servants (Awliyā') is not materialism. «أتمدّونن» (Men of God do not trade themselves for money, because they are masters of the world, not its captives.)

2- Men of God are sharp-sighted, vigilant, and decisive. They see the corrupt motives behind gifts, avoid them, and rebuke and reproach the owners of those motives. «أتمدّونن بمال»

3- Accept and respond to gifts in the best manner. «اذاحیّیتم بتحیّة فحیّوا باحسن منها»(50) However, this does not apply in cases where the gift constitutes a bribe. «أتمدّونن»

4- The disbeliever assumes everyone follows his own creed (or nature). «أتمدّونن بمال»

5- Worldly wealth is insignificant. «بمالٍ» In literary terms, the tanwīn (nunation) is for belittlement (contempt).

6- Men of God know that blessings come from Him. «فما آتانى اللّه»

7- Sometimes, expressing one's own power (or status) is necessary. «فما آتانى اللّه خیر ممّا آتاكم»

8- The best leverage for rejecting unlawful wealth is attention to Divine favors. «أتمدّونن بمال فما آتانى اللّه خیر ممّا آتاكم»

9- Allah's chosen servants achieve tranquility through Divine favors. «فما آتانى اللّه خیر ممّا آتاكم» Whereas materialistic people find joy in material things. «بل أنتم بهدیّتكم تفرحون»

10- Knowledge and wisdom are better than wealth. «فما آتانى اللّه خیر ممّا آتاكم»

11- After logic, it is the turn of demonstrating power. «فلنأتینّهم»

12- Speak firmly against those who rely solely on power. «فلنأتینّهم»

13- Jihād (struggle/holy war) was also relevant in previous religions. «فلنأتینّهم»

14- Propagation (Tablīgh) and guidance (Irshād) are ineffective without migration (Hijrah) and power. «فلنأتینّهم»

15- Whoever foregoes materialism can speak with power and defend the truth. «اِرجع الیهم فلنأتینّهم»

16- A leader must have sufficient confidence in his armed forces. «بجنودٍ لاقِبَلَ لهم بها»

17- The military power of the people of truth must exceed the power of the people of falsehood. «بجنودٍ لاقِبَل لهم»

18- It is permissible to humiliate and disgrace the governments of disbelief (Kufr) and polytheism (Shirk). «اَذلّةً وهم صاغرون» 50) Nisa, 86.

Ayahs

قَالَ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمَلَؤُا۟ أَيُّكُمْ يَأْتِينِى بِعَرْشِهَا قَبْلَ أَن يَأْتُونِى مُسْلِمِينَ (۳۸)
He said, ‘O [members of the] elite! Which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?’ (38)
قَالَ عِفْرِيتٌ مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ أَنَا۠ ءَاتِيكَ بِهِۦ قَبْلَ أَن تَقُومَ مِن مَّقَامِكَ ۖ وَإِنِّى عَلَيْهِ لَقَوِىٌّ أَمِينٌ (۳۹)
An afreet from among the jinn said, ‘I will bring it to you before you rise from your place. I have the power to do it and am trustworthy.’ (39)

Key Points

The word "عِفریت" (Ifrit) contains the meaning of intensity and power. After the return of Bilqis's envoys, along with their gifts, and the Queen of Sheba's realization that Solomon was not merely a king; the Queen decided to come to Solomon personally to see the situation up close. Solomon became aware of Bilqis's movement and prepared himself for a display of power.

Messages

1- Solomon's entourage possessed extraordinary or miraculous power. «ایّكم»

2- Keep the field open for healthy competition. «ایّكم»

3- Utilize the abilities of others in appropriate circumstances. «ایّكم یأتینى»

4- The criterion is merit and ability, not gender. «ایّكم یأتینى» Although Solomon's entourage included Jinn, birds, and humans, the criterion for superiority was the power to bring the throne, not the gender of the one who brought it.

5- If we take the throne and crown away from a person, their submission is easy. «یأتینى بعرشها»

6- The Quran accepts the issue of Tayy al-Ard (folding of the earth) (51). «یأتینى بعرشها قبل أن...»

7- The display of power must be commensurate with the audience. «عرشها»

8- To guide and lead others, use their own resources. «عرشها»

9- The victor is the one who acts before the enemy. «قبل أن یأتونى»

10- Extraordinary deeds must be accompanied by a sublime goal. «یأتینى بعرشها - یأتونى مسلمین»

11- Prophet Solomon had knowledge of the unseen (Ilm al-Ghayb). «یأتونى مسلمین»

12- In a successful system and government, all forces must be utilized in their proper place. «قال عِفریت من الجنّ»

13- The Jinn can help humans. «قال عِفریت من الجنّ»

14- Announce your merits to others. «أنَا آتیك»

15- Speed, power, and trustworthiness are prerequisites for accomplishing great tasks. «قبل أن تَقوم من مقامك - لَقوىّ اَمین»

51) Tayy al-Ard (folding of the earth) is a type of miracle and divine favor, where instead of traversing a long distance, the earth is folded beneath one's feet, allowing them to reach their destination in a short time. (Mu'in Dictionary)

Ayahs

قَالَ ٱلَّذِى عِندَهُۥ عِلْمٌ مِّنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ أَنَا۠ ءَاتِيكَ بِهِۦ قَبْلَ أَن يَرْتَدَّ إِلَيْكَ طَرْفُكَ ۚ فَلَمَّا رَءَاهُ مُسْتَقِرًّا عِندَهُۥ قَالَ هَـٰذَا مِن فَضْلِ رَبِّى لِيَبْلُوَنِىٓ ءَأَشْكُرُ أَمْ أَكْفُرُ ۖ وَمَن شَكَرَ فَإِنَّمَا يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ رَبِّى غَنِىٌّ كَرِيمٌ (٤٠)
The one who had knowledge of the Book said, ‘I will bring it to you in the twinkling of an eye.’ So when he saw it set near him, he said, ‘This is by the grace of my Lord, to test me if I will give thanks or be ungrateful. Whoever gives thanks, gives thanks only for his own sake. And whoever is ungrateful [should know that] my Lord is indeed all-sufficient, all-generous.’ (40)
قَالَ نَكِّرُوا۟ لَهَا عَرْشَهَا نَنظُرْ أَتَهْتَدِىٓ أَمْ تَكُونُ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَهْتَدُونَ (٤۱)
He said, ‘Disguise her throne for her, so that we may see whether she is discerning or if she is one of the undiscerning ones.’ (41)

Key Points

The person who possessed a portion of the knowledge of the Book was "Asif ibn Barkhiya," the minister and nephew of Prophet Sulayman (Solomon), as has been narrated from Imam Hadi عليه السلام.(52) And Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The Greatest Name of Allah (Ism al-A'zam) consists of seventy-three letters, and Asif ibn Barkhiya demonstrated such power merely by knowing one letter.(53) Regarding what is meant by the knowledge of the Book ('Ilm al-Kitab), it has been said that it refers either to awareness of the Divine Books, or the Preserved Tablet (Lawh al-Mahfuz), or the Greatest Name (Ism al-A'zam).

Question: How did "Asif ibn Barkhiya" move the throne and take possession of it without the owner's permission? Answer: This action was due to the authority (Wilayah) of the Prophets over people's possessions and the importance of guiding Bilqis and her people.

Disbelief (Kufr) is of several types: one is the denial of Allah, and another is the ingratitude for blessings (Kufran al-Ni'am). Gratitude (Shukr) to Allah has various forms: gratitude with the tongue, with the heart, and with action.

Virtues of Amir al-Mu'minin and the Ahl al-Bayt عليهم السلام

  • In the Ayah under discussion, it states that the person who possessed a portion of the knowledge of the Book said: O Sulayman! Before your eyelid blinks, I will bring the throne of the Queen of Saba' from her country here. However, in the last Ayah of Surah Ar-Ra'd, Allah tells His Prophet: The disbelievers do not accept your prophethood; tell them, "Sufficient is Allah and he who possesses the entire knowledge of the Book as a witness between me and you." In narrations, we read that the one who possesses the entire knowledge of the Book is 'Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام. If someone who knows a portion of the knowledge of the Book can bring Bilqis's throne in an instant, then what power does the one who knows the entire knowledge of the Book possess throughout his life?
  • Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: The knowledge of the one who possessed a portion of the knowledge, compared to the knowledge of 'Ali عليه السلام, is like the amount of water on the wing of a fly compared to the ocean.(54)
  • In narrations, we read that Imam Sadiq عليه السلام pointed to his blessed chest and said: «و عندنا واللّه علم الكتاب كله» (And by Allah, the entire knowledge of the Book is with us).(55)
  • In many narrations, we read that the infallible Imams عليهم السلام would appear in various regions outside the constraints of time and space. For example: Imam Jawad عليه السلام traveled from Medina to Tus at the moment of his father's martyrdom. Imam Kadhim عليه السلام came out of the Baghdad prison and appeared in Medina. Imam Sajjad عليه السلام, during captivity, went to Karbala and buried the body of his father, Imam Husayn عليه السلام. Imam Husayn عليه السلام, before his martyrdom, took a handful of earth from Karbala and gave it to Umm Salama in Medina.(56) Therefore, Tayy al-Ard (folding the earth/instantaneous travel) and lightning-fast movement have precedents for the Imams.

(52) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. (53) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 230. (54) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. (55) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. (56) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The most knowledgeable figures were present in Sulayman's cabinet. «عنده علم من الكتاب»

2- Man can dominate nature and natural laws. «أنا آتیك»

3- Human power can be greater than that of the Jinn. «عِفریت من الجنّ - الّذى عنده علم...»

4- In certain situations, one must demonstrate one's power. «أنَا آتیك»

5- Knowledge is the source of power. With scientific power, instead of just hearing sound and seeing images, one can move objects and beings themselves. (Man possesses talents, abilities, and secrets that have not yet been discovered). «أنَا آتیك»

He who is knowledgeable is capable, From knowledge, the heart of the old becomes young.

6- In assigning missions, musara'ah (speed of action) is an advantage, just as auctioning in sales and tendering in purchasing are factors of competition and value. «أنَا آتیك به قبل أن یرتدّ الیك طرفك»

7- Claimants of knowledge and power must practically prove their claims. «فلمّا رآه مستقرّاً»

8- The friends of Allah (Awliya') attribute blessings to Him. «هذا من فضل ربّى»

9- We should not consider Divine blessings as our inherent right. «فضل ربّى»

10- We should not become arrogant about our knowledge and power. «هذا من فضل ربّى»

11- We should express Divine blessings verbally. «هذا من فضل ربّى»

12- The giving or taking away of blessings is for our growth. «ربّى»

13- Divine blessings are a means for servitude and trial, not for mere enjoyment and pleasure-seeking. «لیبلونى ءاشكر»

14- Prophets are also tested. «لیبلونى»

15- Man is free (to choose), not compelled. «مَن شكر... و مَن كفر»

16- Man is dependent on his own actions. «و مَن شكر فانّما یشكُر لنفسه»

17- Allah does not need our gratitude. «مَن شكر... ومَن كفر فانّ ربّى غنى كریم» Divine blessings stem from His endless grace and kindness, and even if we are ungrateful, He shows kindness to us according to the requirement of His Lordship (Rububiyyah).

Gratitude for a blessing increases your blessing, Ingratitude for a blessing takes it out of your hand.

18- Flattery is forbidden in the method of invitation (Da'wah). «و مَن كفر فانّ ربّى غنى»

19- Wealth (Self-Sufficiency) and generosity together constitute value. «غنى كریم»

20- The friends of Allah (Awliya') have authority (Wilayah) over people's possessions. «نَكّروا لها»

21- Sometimes, change and transformation are permissible for the purpose of testing intelligence. «نكّروا لها... ننظر أتهتدى»

Ayahs

فَلَمَّا جَآءَتْ قِيلَ أَهَـٰكَذَا عَرْشُكِ ۖ قَالَتْ كَأَنَّهُۥ هُوَ ۚ وَأُوتِينَا ٱلْعِلْمَ مِن قَبْلِهَا وَكُنَّا مُسْلِمِينَ (٤۲)
So when she came, it was said [to her], ‘Is your throne like this one?’ She said, ‘It seems to be the same, and we were informed before it, and we had submitted.’ (42)
وَصَدَّهَا مَا كَانَت تَّعْبُدُ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهَا كَانَتْ مِن قَوْمٍ كَـٰفِرِينَ (٤۳)
She had been barred [from the way of Allah] by what she used to worship besides Allah, for she belonged to a faithless people. (43)

Key Points

The phrase «و اوتینا العلم» (and we were given knowledge) is apparently a continuation of the speech of Bilqis (which we included in the translation), but it is also possible that it is the speech of Solomon (Sulayman) and his companions, who might have said: We were given knowledge before Bilqis, and we were Muslims before her. (57)

Question: Since Bilqis had become aware of the truthfulness of Solomon, why did she not submit sooner?

Answer: The society and environment of polytheism prevented her from breaking the barrier and submitting, as the Quran states: «وصَدّها ما كانت تَعبد من دون اللّه اِنّها كانت من قومٍ كافرین» (And she was prevented [from faith] by what she used to worship instead of Allah. Indeed, she was from a disbelieving people.)

57) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr al-Razi.

Messages

1- In initial encounters, do not state the final word first. «اهكذا - كانه هو» (He did not say: «هذا عرشك» [Is this your throne?], nor did she say: «انّه هو» [Indeed, it is it].)

2- A school of thought (or doctrine) is valuable only if it is based on knowledge. «و اوتینا العلم من قبلها و كنّا مسلمین»

3- The environment, society, and superstitious beliefs are obstacles to true faith. «و صدّها ما كانت»

4- A bad past record is not proof of a bad future. «انّها كانت من قوم كافرین»

Ayah

قِيلَ لَهَا ٱدْخُلِى ٱلصَّرْحَ ۖ فَلَمَّا رَأَتْهُ حَسِبَتْهُ لُجَّةً وَكَشَفَتْ عَن سَاقَيْهَا ۚ قَالَ إِنَّهُۥ صَرْحٌ مُّمَرَّدٌ مِّن قَوَارِيرَ ۗ قَالَتْ رَبِّ إِنِّى ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِى وَأَسْلَمْتُ مَعَ سُلَيْمَـٰنَ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٤٤)
It was said to her, ‘Enter the palace.’ So when she saw it, she supposed it to be a pool of water, and she bared her shanks. He said, ‘It is a palace paved with crystal.’ She said, ‘My Lord! Indeed I have wronged myself, and I submit with Solomon to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.’ (44)

Key Points

The word "صَرح" (ṣarḥ) means a large space or palace. "لُجّة" (lujjah) means turbulent and dense, and "مُمَرّد" (mumarrad) means smooth.

Messages

1- The status of prophethood is not incompatible with majesty, splendor, and governance. «قیل لها ادخلى الصّرح»

2- In guidance and propagation (Da'wah), everyone must be guided in a specific manner. «قیل لها ادخلى الصّرح» We should not deal with the affluent in a way that makes them imagine faith means poverty.

3- Material resources must be placed in the service of propagating religion. «قیل لها ادخلى الصّرح» With Solomonic objectives, one can utilize industry and material resources to help guide and instruct others.

4- Treat non-believing guests with good manners. «قیل لها ادخلى الصّرح»

5- Preserve the dignity of individuals during hospitality. «قیل لها ادخلى الصّرح»

6- Sometimes a person sees, but diagnoses incorrectly (an optical illusion). «حسبته لجّة»

7- Compel the non-believing palace dwellers to submit before the industry, innovation, art, and beauty of your system. «صرح ممرّد من قواریر»

8- The history of mirror work and the use of glass in architecture dates back to the time of Prophet Sulayman (Solomon). «صرح ممرّد من قواریر»

9- Accepting the truth is a sign of freedom, not weakness. «ربّ ظلمتُ نفسى»

10- Dependence on and attachment to other than Allah and the worship of other than Him—whoever or whatever it may be—is oppression (zulm). «ظلمتُ نفسى»

11- True repentance is compensating for the past through confession and traversing the path of the future with the light of Divine guidance. «ظلمتُ نفسى و أسلمتُ مع سلیمان»

12- Do not let having a name, wealth, title, and entourage enslave you when confessing the truth and changing your belief. «ظلمتُ نفسى و أسلمتُ»

13- Development (Takamul) has stages: The first stage is exiting darkness, and the subsequent stage is entering light. «ظلمتُ نفسى و أسلمتُ مع سلیمان»

14- The spirit of faith is submission (Taslim). Sulayman referred to it in His invitation: «وأتونى مسلمین» (and come to me in submission), and the Queen of Sheba also says at the end: «أسلمتُ» (I have submitted).

15- Faith is submission to the Creator of existence, not to creation, even if it is Sulayman. «أسلمتُ للّه»

16- A woman is independent in choosing and declaring her belief. «أسلمتُ للّه»

17- Faith alone is not enough; accompanying the Awliya (saints) and the Prophets safeguards a person. «أسلمتُ مع سلیمان للّه»

18- In contrast to political, economic, military, and familial ties, Divine and sacred ties are also found. «مع سلیمان»

19- Gold and adornments do not satisfy virtuous individuals; their lost objective is access to the Source of existence. «أسلمتُ... للّه ربّ العالمین»

20- The goal of the governance of the Prophets is to invite towards Allah, not territorial expansion. The Queen of Sheba also understood this truth, hence she said at the end of her speech: «أسلمتُ مع سلیمان للّه ربّ العالمین»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَـٰلِحًا أَنِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ فَإِذَا هُمْ فَرِيقَانِ يَخْتَصِمُونَ (٤٥)
Certainly We sent to Thamud Salih, their brother, [with the summons:] ‘Worship Allah!’ But thereat they became two groups contending with each other. (45)
قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ لِمَ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِٱلسَّيِّئَةِ قَبْلَ ٱلْحَسَنَةِ ۖ لَوْلَا تَسْتَغْفِرُونَ ٱللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (٤٦)
He said, ‘O My people! Why do you press for evil sooner than for good? Why do you not plead to Allah for forgiveness so that you may receive His mercy?’ (46)

Key Points

Prophet Salih used to warn the people and frighten them with Allah's punishment, but they would say: «یا صالحُ ائتنا بما تَعِدُنا» (58) "Bring upon us that punishment which you promise us." In this Ayah, Salih says: Why do you seek evil instead of good? Just as the disbelievers also sought the hastening of punishment from Prophet Hud عليه السلام and the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: A group affirmed the call of Prophet Salih, and a group denied it. (59) Although all the Prophets had an emotional and brotherly interaction with the people, the fact that the word «اخاهم» (their brother) is used only for some of them might suggest that some Prophets, in addition to their compassionate interaction, were also relatives of their people.

58) Al-A'raf, 77. 59) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The interaction of the Prophets with the people was brotherly. «اَخاهم»

2- Invitation to Monotheism (Tawhid) is the priority of the Prophets' call. «أنِ اعبدوا اللّه»

3- Do not expect all people to accept your logic. «فاذا هم فریقان»

4- The conflict between truth (Haqq) and falsehood (Batil) is perpetual. «یختصمون»

5- Keep the path of repentance (Tawbah) open for the return of the sinners. «لولا تستغفرون»

6- The Prophets are compassionate towards the people. «یا قوم ... لولا تستغفرون اللّه لعلّكم ترحمون»

7- Seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) is the basis for attracting Divine mercy. «تستغفرون اللّه لعلّكم ترحمون»

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ ٱطَّيَّرْنَا بِكَ وَبِمَن مَّعَكَ ۚ قَالَ طَـٰٓئِرُكُمْ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ تُفْتَنُونَ (٤۷)
They said, ‘We take you and those who are with you for a bad omen.’ He said, ‘Your bad omens are from Allah. Indeed, you are a people being tested.’ (47)
وَكَانَ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ تِسْعَةُ رَهْطٍ يُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا يُصْلِحُونَ (٤۸)
There were nine persons in the city who caused corruption in the land and did not set things right. (48)

Key Points

The word raht (رَهط) refers to a group whose members have a warm relationship among themselves. (60) The word taṭayyur (تَطیُّر) comes from ṭayr (طیر), meaning bird. When traveling, the Arabs would release a bird; if it flew to the right (they considered this a good omen and) they would travel, and if it flew to the left (they considered it a bad omen and) they would cancel the journey. (61) In Islam, taking a bad omen (known as ṭīrah) is considered shirk (polytheism). Taking a bad omen means abandoning the primary reasons and resorting to superstitions. Taking a bad omen regarding the prophets is frequently mentioned in the Quran: The Pharaoh's people attributed misfortunes to Moses; «یطّیّروا بموسى» (62) They said to Jesus (عليه السلام): «انّا تطیّرنا بكم» (63) In this ayah (verse) too, it states that they said to Salih: «اطّیّرنا بك» The phrase «بل انتم قوم تفتنون» has been interpreted in several ways:

A: You have been deceived by treacherous factors, which is why you resist in this manner. (Based on fitnah meaning deception and trickery) B: You are being tested by bitter events. (If the intent of fitnah is trial and test) C: You are being punished due to obstinacy. (Based on the intent of fitnah being punishment/chastisement)

60) Al-Tahqīq fī Kalimāt al-Qur’ān. 61) Tafsir Kashshāf. 62) Al-A‘rāf, 131. 63) Yā Sīn, 18.

Messages

1- The Prophet of Islam and the Muslims should know how the previous prophets and their companions were humiliated, so they should endure the hardships. «قالوا اطّیّرنا»

2- Taking omens has a very long history. (Whoever does not believe in Allah and Divine wisdom turns to superstitions). «اطّیّرنا»

3- In a system where knowledge, refinement, logic, and revelation do not govern, figures who are sources of blessing are introduced as sources of misfortune. «اطّیّرنا بك»

4- The presence of the prophets does not mean the elimination of natural and everyday problems. «اطّیّرنا بك»

5- Hardships and unpleasant events each have wise reasons that Allah has established. «طائركم عنداللّه»

6- Interaction with the prophets is a Divine test. «تفتنون»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ تَقَاسَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ لَنُبَيِّتَنَّهُۥ وَأَهْلَهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَقُولَنَّ لِوَلِيِّهِۦ مَا شَهِدْنَا مَهْلِكَ أَهْلِهِۦ وَإِنَّا لَصَـٰدِقُونَ (٤۹)
They said, ‘Swear by Allah that we will attack him and his family by night. Then we will tell his heir that we were not present at the murder of his family and that we speak the truth.’ (49)

Key Points

"لَنبیّتنّه" (We will surely attack him by night) comes from the word tabyīt, meaning assassination attempt and nocturnal assault. In Islam, an oath is valuable and fulfilling it is obligatory only if it relates to performing a worthy deed or abandoning a reprehensible act. Therefore, if a person swears an oath to strike an oppressed person or support an oppressor, that oath holds no religious or legal value. This verse reminds us of the oath taken by the disbelievers of Mecca, who decided to launch a night raid and martyr the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله in his bed. However, the Noble Prophet had placed Imam Ali عليه السلام in his place, and the enemy's plot was foiled.

Messages

1- Secret statements are revealed centuries later. «قالوا تقاسموا»

2- The enemy misuses religious sanctities. «تقاسموا باللّه»

3- The most criminal individuals—who are the killers of the prophets—accept Allah in their hearts and organize their wicked deeds in His name. «تقاسموا باللّه»

4- Individuals or groups of falsehood unite and swear oaths for their common goals. «تقاسموا»

5- There are many lessons in history. (Swearing by the name of Allah to kill the Prophet of Allah!) «تقاسموا باللّه لَنبیّتنه» (Just as the Khawarij struck and martyred the Wali (Guardian) of Allah while he was worshipping Allah, inside the House of Allah, during the Month of Allah, with the intention of seeking closeness to Allah, on the nights of Qadr and the dawn of the nineteenth of Ramadan.)

6- Using the darkness of night to execute plots has a long history. «لَنبیّتنّه»

7- Prophets and their helpers have been exposed to the most severe plots. «لَنبیّتنّه و اهله»

8- Human cruelty knows no bounds. (Killing a prophet, killing the prophet's relatives, and launching a night raid). «لنبیّتنّه و اهله»

9- Retaliation (Qisas) is a rational law and has existed in all heavenly religions. «نقولنّ لولیّه»

10- The criminal covers up his sin through pretense and justification. «ما شهدنا»

11- Do not trust everyone who claims sincerity and champions honesty. «لنقولنّ... انّا لصادقون»

12- Even the killers of the prophets consider sincerity (truthfulness) to be an honor and a value. «انّا لصادقون»

Ayahs

وَمَكَرُوا۟ مَكْرًا وَمَكَرْنَا مَكْرًا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (٥٠)
They devised a plot, and We [too] devised a plan, but they were not aware. (50)
فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ مَكْرِهِمْ أَنَّا دَمَّرْنَـٰهُمْ وَقَوْمَهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (٥۱)
So observe how was the outcome of their plotting, as We destroyed them and all their people. (51)

Key Points

The destruction of the people of Thamūd is mentioned in the Quran with several expressions: A: Earthquake. «فاخذتهم الرَّجفة» (64) B: Thunderbolt/Blast. «فاخذتهم الصاعقة» (65) C: Cry/Shout. «و اَخَذ الّذین ظلموا الصَیحة» (66) Of course, there is no impediment to all three punishments occurring at the same time. One example of Divine plotting (Makar) is granting respite and blessings to sinners so that they become preoccupied, and as soon as their measure is full, they are suddenly afflicted by Divine wrath.

(64) Al-A'rāf, 78. (65) Adh-Dhāriyāt, 44. (66) Hūd, 67.

Messages

1- Divine punishments are commensurate with the human crime. «مكروا - مكرنا»

2- Those who plot against the friends (Awliyā) of Allah have Allah as their opponent. «مكروا مكراً و مكرنا مكراً»

3- In the face of the schemes of the disbelievers (Kuffār), you must also be people of planning and strategy. «مكروا مكراً و مكرنا مكراً»

4- Allah knows the planning of the people, but the people do not know His planning. «وهم لایشعرون»

5- The rod of God (Divine punishment) is silent (comes without warning). «مكرنا... و هم لایشعرون»

6- History is based on a specific law and tradition (Sunnah), and careful study of it is beneficial for other eras. «فانظر كیف كان عاقبة مكرهم»

7- The Divine tradition (Sunnah) is founded upon the victory of truth (Haqq). «فانظر كیف كان عاقبة مكرهم»
