8- Do not judge hastily; do not be deceived by the successes that some achieve through plotting, but rather pay attention to their ultimate outcome. «عاقبة مكرهم»

9- The enemies' readiness to commit crime is a cause for Divine wrath, even if they do not achieve their wicked goals. (The enemies swore together to kill the Prophet and lay in wait, but they did not kill him, yet they themselves were destroyed). «دمّرناهم»

10- Following the corruptors (Mufsidūn) incurs punishment. «دمّرناهم و قومهم»

Ayahs

فَتِلْكَ بُيُوتُهُمْ خَاوِيَةًۢ بِمَا ظَلَمُوٓا۟ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ (٥۲)
So there lay their houses, fallen in ruin because of their wrongdoing. There is indeed a sign in that for a people who have knowledge. (52)
وَأَنجَيْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ يَتَّقُونَ (٥۳)
And We delivered those who had faith and were Godwary. (53)

Key Points

The word "خاویة" (Khāwiyah) means both downfall, annihilation, and ruin, and also becoming empty of inhabitants.

Messages

1- Historical ruins that serve as lessons should be preserved for future generations. «فتلك بیوتهم»

2- Punishment is not exclusive to the Hereafter; sometimes oppressors receive punishment in this world. «فتلك بیوتهم خاویة»

3- Lessons and signs are not sufficient on their own; the motivation to learn lessons must exist within people. «لآیة لقوم یعلمون»

4- The destiny of man is tied to his deeds. «خاویة بما ظلموا - انجینا الّذین آمنوا»

5- Divine wrath does not consume the innocent along with the guilty; therefore, the pious are exempted and saved. «و انجینا الّذین آمنوا و كانوا یتّقون»

6- The effects of faith and piety are not exclusive to the Hereafter; the people of piety also see the results of their deeds in this world. «و انجینا الّذین آمنوا و كانوا یتّقون»

7- Faith is effective when accompanied by continuous piety. «آمنوا و كانوا یتّقون»

Ayahs

وَلُوطًا إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِۦٓ أَتَأْتُونَ ٱلْفَـٰحِشَةَ وَأَنتُمْ تُبْصِرُونَ (٥٤)
[We also sent] Lot, when he said to his people, ‘What! Do you commit this indecency while you look on? (54)
أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ ٱلرِّجَالَ شَهْوَةً مِّن دُونِ ٱلنِّسَآءِ ۚ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ تَجْهَلُونَ (٥٥)
Do you approach men with [sexual] desire instead of women?! Indeed, you are a senseless lot!’ (55)

Key Points

The people of Lot committed sodomy (liwāṭ) in front of each other and in public gatherings. In Ayah 29 of Surah Al-Ankabūt (The Spider), we read: «و تأتون فى نادیكم المنكر» (And you commit the evil deed in your gatherings), meaning you commit evil in your assembly and public meeting. In this Ayah (of Surah An-Naml), He also says: «و انتم تبصرون», meaning you commit sin in front of each other, or with insight and awareness.

Sodomy (liwāṭ) is among the major sins (Kabā'ir), because the subsequent verses state that it brings divine punishment (ʿadhāb Ilāhī).

In other verses of the Holy Quran, it is mentioned that a spouse is for tranquility, raising offspring, and cooperation in life. But here, only the issue of lust (shahwah) is mentioned, because the people of Lot had no goal other than lust. «شهوة من دون النساء» (lust instead of women).

Messages

1- Prophets and reforming leaders of society strongly combat the prevalent sins of their time. «أتأتون الفاحشة»

2- Awaken consciences through critical questioning. «أتأتون الفاحشة»

3- One of the duties of the Prophets is to combat evil deeds (munkarāt). «أتأتون الفاحشة»

4- The prohibition of sodomy (liwāṭ) is not exclusive to Islam. «أتأتون الفاحشة»

5- Public and conscious sin has greater ugliness, punishment, and criticism. «و انتم تبصرون»

6- The ugliness of sodomy (liwāṭ) is clear to everyone. «و انتم تبصرون»

7- In enjoining the good and forbidding the evil (Nahy ʿanil Munkar), address public evil deeds. «و انتم تبصرون»

8- The fulfillment of lust must be through legitimate means. «أئنّكم لتأتون الرجال شهوة...»

9- Deviation from the natural and lawful path in sexual matters is ignorance and foolishness. «بل انتم قوم تجهلون»

10- Homosexuality stems from a lack of culture/refinement. Knowledge and learning that do not restrain a person from sin are ignorance. «لتأتون الرجال... انتم قوم تجهلون»

Ayah

۞ فَمَا كَانَ جَوَابَ قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوٓا۟ أَخْرِجُوٓا۟ ءَالَ لُوطٍ مِّن قَرْيَتِكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ أُنَاسٌ يَتَطَهَّرُونَ (٥٦)
But the only answer of his people was that they said, ‘Expel Lot’s family from your town! They are indeed a puritanical lot.’ (56)

Messages

1- Since the criminals lack logic, they resort to force against the prophets. «اَخرجوا»

2- In a polluted environment, chastity is a crime. «اَخرجوا آل لوط... انّهم اُناس یتطهّرون»

3- Forbidding the evil sometimes carries the price of being exiled. «اَخرجوا آل لوط»

4- Silence in the face of sin is forbidden; at the very least, we should restrict the space for the sinner. «اَخرجوا آل لوط»

5- Freedom to sin is the ideology of the people of Lot. «اَخرجوا آل لوط»

6- The history of the exile of men of God dates back to the time of Abraham and Lot. «اَخرجوا»

7- Since the innate nature (fitrah) of the sinner is pure, he therefore sees himself as the criminal and others as pure. «انّهم اُناس یتطهّرون»

8- The environment does not compel man to sin, because even during the spread of immorality, a group lives purely. «اُناس یتطهّرون»

Ayahs

فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَأَهْلَهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱمْرَأَتَهُۥ قَدَّرْنَـٰهَا مِنَ ٱلْغَـٰبِرِينَ (٥۷)
So We delivered him and his family, except his wife. We ordained her to be among those who remained behind. (57)
وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّطَرًا ۖ فَسَآءَ مَطَرُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ (٥۸)
Then We poured down upon them a rain [of stones]. Evil was that rain for those who had been warned! (58)

Key Points

The word ghābirīn (غابرین) means those who remain behind and those who are destroyed. This word has been used seven times in the Quran, and all seven instances refer to the wife of Lot. The intent of the rain [mentioned here] is hard clay, based on the Ayah: «و اَمطرنا علیهم حجارة من سِجّیل» (67) (And We rained upon them stones of baked clay). Allah has repeatedly promised the salvation of the believers and the good outcome for the pious, and He has demonstrated its realization throughout the history of the Prophets and nations, including:

A: «انّا لننصر رسلنا والّذین آمنوا» (68) We shall certainly help Our Messengers and those who have believed. B: «حقّاً علینا ننج المؤمنین» (69) It is incumbent upon Us to save the people of faith.

(67) Al-Hijr, 74. (68) Ghāfir, 51. (69) Yūnus, 103.

Messages

1- The fruit of piety (taqwa) and chastity in this world is salvation from Divine wrath. «فانجیناه» (So We saved him)

2- Divine salvation occurs through natural means. (Lot left the area by Allah’s command in order to be saved). «فانجیناه»

3- Divine leaders are under the protection of Allah. «فانجیناه»

4- A woman is independent in choosing her belief; the family system, living environment, and economic dependence on her husband do not compel her in her choice of belief and action. «الاّامراته» (Except his wife)

5- Affiliation is not a factor for salvation; merit is necessary. «الاّامراته»

6- In punishing and penalizing offenders, do not be influenced by relationships. «الاّامراته»

7- In interactions, we must examine the account of every person separately; the deviation of a wife does not harm the purity of her husband. «فانجیناه... الاّامراته» (The wife of a Prophet can be destined for Hellfire).

8- Sometimes the wife is an infiltrator working for the opponents. (Whenever an opportunity for sodomy arose—for example, if handsome guests came to the house of Prophet Lot—this woman would inform the people and was content with the sins of others). «الاّامراته»

9- Divine decrees depend on the actions of the individual himself. «قدّرناها» (We decreed it)

10- Those who cut off procreation through sodomy have their own lineage cut off by being rained upon with stones. «امطرنا» (We rained down)

11- Changing the path of marriage to homosexuality, and altering the law of creation, causes the rain of mercy to change into the rain of punishment. «فساء مطر المنذرین» (Evil was the rain of those who had been warned)

12- Divine punishments occur after the completion of the argument (Hujjah). «مطر المنذرین» (The rain of those who had been warned)

Ayah

قُلِ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَسَلَـٰمٌ عَلَىٰ عِبَادِهِ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱصْطَفَىٰٓ ۗ ءَآللَّهُ خَيْرٌ أَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (٥۹)
Say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, and Peace be to the servants whom He has chosen.’ Is Allah better, or the partners they ascribe [to Him]? (59)

Messages

1- The punishment of the criminals and the salvation of the believers are among the Divine favors, and thanks must be rendered for them. «فساء مطر المنذرین - قل الحمدللّه»

2- We must learn the method of giving thanks from Allah. «قل الحمدللّه»

3- The praise of Allah's servants and peace upon them are mentioned alongside the praise of Allah. «الحمدللّه و سلام على عباده»

4- Let us remember Allah, «قل الحمدللّه», so that Allah may also keep our memory alive. «سلام على عباده»

5- Do not forget the remembrance of Allah's chosen friends. «سلام على عباده»

6- Allah's chosen friends are alive and receive our greetings (salām). «سلام على عباده»

7- Let us entrust our hearts to Allah and become His servants so that we may receive the salām (peace/greetings) of Allah and the Messenger. «سلام على عباده»

8- Human servitude (servanthood) is the criterion for Divine selection. «عباده الّذین اصطفى»

9- Awaken consciences through questioning. «ءاللّه خیر امّا یشركون»

Ayah

أَمَّنْ خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَأَنزَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَنۢبَتْنَا بِهِۦ حَدَآئِقَ ذَاتَ بَهْجَةٍ مَّا كَانَ لَكُمْ أَن تُنۢبِتُوا۟ شَجَرَهَآ ۗ أَءِلَـٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ يَعْدِلُونَ (٦٠)
Is He who created the heavens and the earth, and sends down for you water from the sky, whereby We grow delightful gardens, whose trees you could never cause to grow...? What! Is there a god besides Allah? Indeed, they are a lot who equate [others with Allah]. (60)

Key Points

"حدائق" (Hada'iq) is the plural of "حدیقه" (Hadiqah), meaning a garden that has a wall, similar to the pupil (Hadaqah) of the eye.

Messages

1- Reflection upon creation is the best way to reach Allah. «أمّن خلق»

2- Every tree and plant that grows is under the supervision of Allah and by His will. «اَنبتنا»

3- The exercise of Divine power occurs through ordinary means (causes). «اَنبتنا به»

4- Delightful beauty is a value. «ذات بهجةٍ»

5- We should speak in a way that everyone understands. (Not everyone understands the properties and secrets of plants, but everyone perceives the freshness and beauty of greenery and flowers.) «حدائق ذات بهجةٍ»

6- We realize the power of Allah when we realize our own inability—even to grow a single tree. «ما كان لكم ان تنبتوا»

7- We should awaken consciences with meaningful questions. «أمّن خلق... ءالهٌ مع اللّه»

8- Prevent doubts (or ambiguities). The creation of the heavens and the earth has no claimant, but cultivation and farming do have claimants. Some say: We made the area green and prosperous. Therefore, Allah says: «ما كان لكم ان تنبتوا شجرها» and in another place He says: «ءانتم تَزرعونه ام نحن الزّارعون»(70) Is it you who cultivate it, or are We the Cultivators?

9- Monotheism (Tawhid) is the straight path, and polytheism (Shirk) is the deviant path. «یعدلون» (70) Al-Waqi'ah, 64.

Ayahs

أَمَّن جَعَلَ ٱلْأَرْضَ قَرَارًا وَجَعَلَ خِلَـٰلَهَآ أَنْهَـٰرًا وَجَعَلَ لَهَا رَوَٰسِىَ وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَ ٱلْبَحْرَيْنِ حَاجِزًا ۗ أَءِلَـٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٦۱)
Is He who made the earth an abode [for you], and made rivers [flowing] through it, and set firm mountains for it [’s stability], and set a barrier between the two seas...? What! Is there a god besides Allah? Indeed, most of them do not know. (61)
أَمَّن يُجِيبُ ٱلْمُضْطَرَّ إِذَا دَعَاهُ وَيَكْشِفُ ٱلسُّوٓءَ وَيَجْعَلُكُمْ خُلَفَآءَ ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ أَءِلَـٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ (٦۲)
Is He who answers the call of the distressed [person] when he invokes Him and removes his distress, and makes you successors on the earth...? What! Is there a god besides Allah? Little is the admonition that you take. (62)

Key Points

I have not seen in narrations (Riwāyāt) or commentaries (Tafāsīr) that reciting Ayah 62 plays a role in the acceptance of supplication (Du'a), but people recite it for blessing (tabarrukan) due to the suitability of the Ayah's content, and there is no objection to this.

Intimate communion (Munājāt) with Allah, the Glorious, and private devotion to Him is a commendable and necessary act. Supplication to His court and requesting the resolution of problems, especially during distress, is essential for everyone.

This need was like that of Maryam (Mary), and the pain was such that the child began to speak. Wherever there is pain, there is a remedy. Wherever there is poverty, there is provision. Wherever there is a problem, the answer goes there. Wherever there is lowliness, water flows there. Before the Truth (Allah), one cry born of need Is better than a lifetime spent in prostration (Sujud) and prayer (Salat). Abandon force, embrace lamentation. Mercy comes toward lamentation, O poor one. (71)

We read in narrations: The true example of the distressed one (al-muḍṭarr) is Imam al-Zamān (عجّل اللّه تعالى فرجه الشریف), and the worst evil mentioned in "و یكشف السوء" (and removes the evil) is the dominance of the disbelievers, which will be removed during the time of that Imam. The example of "یجعلكم خلفاء" (He makes you successors) is the rule of the righteous during that time. (72)

Wherever our supplication is not answered, there is a reason, such as: A: Our supplication was not a request for good (Khayr), though we thought it was good. (Because Du'a means requesting good.) B: The manner of the request was not serious and accompanied by distress. C: We lacked sincerity (Ikhlāṣ) in the supplication; meaning, if we turned to the court of Allah, we also looked to others with hope.

Of course, sometimes, instead of answering exactly what we ask for, the Wise Allah gives us something similar. Sometimes, instead of granting our request, which is not in our interest according to Allah, He wards off a calamity from us. Sometimes, instead of fulfilling the need in this world, He compensates for it in the Hereafter. And sometimes, instead of showing favor to us, He shows favor to our descendants. All these points have been mentioned in narrations.

Answering the distressed one is a Divine act, but it does not change the Divine tradition (Sunnah). For example, it is Allah's tradition that people must somehow depart from this world, and all human beings are distressed at the moment of death (Iḥtiḍār). If Allah were to give a positive answer to everyone, He would have to abolish the law of death.

The Root of Faith in Allah

The root of faith in Allah is intellect ('Aql) and innate nature (Fiṭrah); however, materialists say that the root of faith in Allah is fear. Meaning, because man sees himself helpless against calamities, when a bitter incident occurs, he imagines a power in his mind and seeks refuge in it. Based on this calculation, materialists consider faith to be born of fear;

But their mistake is that they do not understand the difference between turning toward Allah and the principle of faith in Allah. For example, when we see a dog and feel danger, we look for a stone. But can it be said that the existence of the stone is due to the dog? Similarly, when we are in distress and fear, we turn toward Allah. Can it be said that the principle of faith in Allah is born of fear?

Furthermore, if the source of faith were fear, then whoever is more fearful should be more faithful. Whereas true believers have been the bravest individuals of their time. Also, a person should have no faith during moments when he feels no fear, whereas we accept Allah based on intellect and innate nature even in moments when we have no fear.

In reality, fear and distress remove the veil of heedlessness (Ghaflah) and make us aware of Allah. Therefore, whenever deniers of Allah are sitting in an airplane or a ship and hear news of a crash or sinking, and they become certain that no power can save them, they hope deeply in a single saving power. At that moment, they attach their hearts to one point and one unseen power, and that point is none other than Allah, the Most High.

71) Mawlawi. 72) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- One of the ways to know Allah and His Oneness is to pay attention to a single saving power and to cut off from other powers and means during distress. "أمّن یجیب"

2- The condition for the acceptance of supplication is cutting off hope from others and sincerity (Ikhlāṣ) in the supplication. "یجیب المضطر"

3- Allah is aware of the condition of those who silently request a need from Him, but He loves that His servants express their need verbally. "اذا دعاه"

4- Man has dominion over nature. "یجعلكم خلفاء الارض"

5- Do you pay attention to material powers while in distress?! "ءاله مع اللّه"

Ayah

أَمَّن يَهْدِيكُمْ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتِ ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ وَمَن يُرْسِلُ ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ بُشْرًۢا بَيْنَ يَدَىْ رَحْمَتِهِۦٓ ۗ أَءِلَـٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ تَعَـٰلَى ٱللَّهُ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (٦۳)
Is He who guides you in the darkness of land and sea and who sends the winds as harbingers of His mercy...? What! Is there a god besides Allah? Exalted is Allah above [having] any partners that they ascribe [to Him]. (63)

Key Points

Perhaps the guidance in the darkness of the sea and the land refers to guidance through the stars. Because in another place we read: «و بالنّجم هم یهتدون»(73)

73) Naḥl, 16.

Messages

1- The best way to know Allah is to pay attention to the problems and dead ends of life and the subsequent emergence of solutions and rays of hope through the grace of Allah. «أمّن یهدیكم - من یرسل»

2- The movement of the winds is by His planning. «یرسل الریاح»

3- If we make our conscience the judge, we realize that there is no god besides Him. «ءالهٌ مع اللّه»

4- Every kind of polytheism (shirk) is condemned. «عمّا»

5- Setting up a partner for Allah is an insult to His status. «تعالى اللّه عمّایشركون»

Ayah

أَمَّن يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ وَمَن يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ أَءِلَـٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ قُلْ هَاتُوا۟ بُرْهَـٰنَكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (٦٤)
Is He who originates the creation, then He will bring it back, and who provides for you from the sky and the earth...? What! Is there a god besides Allah? Say, ‘Produce your evidence, if you are truthful.’ (64)

Key Points

From Ayah 60 up to this Ayah, the phrase «ءالهٌ مع اللّه» (Is there any god alongside Allah?) has appeared five consecutive times, which is intended to invalidate polytheism (Shirk) and negate false deities.

Human sustenance is sometimes from the sky, like light, air, and rain; sometimes from the earth, like vegetables and fruits; sometimes from animals, which also indirectly use and grow from the earth; and sometimes from minerals, which are also from the earth. (74)

From the perspective of the Quran, no creature is annihilated; rather, it is only from our perspective that it is lost. (75)

Resurrection (Ma'ad) is not the return of the non-existent, such that some might imagine it to be an impossible task, but rather it is the return of existing beings to their original form, after a change has occurred in their shape.

The repeated verses of the Quran are accompanied by innovation: From Ayah 60 to 64, the phrase «أاله مع اللّه» (Is there any god alongside Allah?) is repeated, but each time it is accompanied by a new sentence:

  • In Ayah 60 we read: «بل هم قوم یعدلون» (Nay, but they are a people who deviate). The polytheists are deviant.
  • In Ayah 61 we read: «بل اكثرهم لایعلمون» (Nay, but most of them do not know). Their deviation is due to ignorance.
  • In Ayah 62 we read: «قلیلا ما تذكّرون» (Little do they remember). And now that they are ignorant, they remember less.
  • In Ayah 63 we read: «تعالى اللّه عمّا یشركون» (Exalted is Allah above what they associate with Him). Allah is exalted above their polytheism.
  • In Ayah 64 we read: «قل هاتوا برهانكم» (Say, "Produce your proof"). These people have resorted to polytheism without proof.

74) Tafsir Namuneh. 75) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The same Power that created in the beginning can return it again. «یبدؤا - یعید»

2- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical (corporeal). «یعیده»

3- Awaken consciences through questioning. «أمّن یبدؤا الخلق - من یرزقكم - ءاله»

4- If your opponents have proof, accept it. «قل هاتوا»

5- Islam is the religion of proof (Burhan) and demands proof even from its opponents. «قل هاتوا برهانكم»

Ayahs

قُل لَّا يَعْلَمُ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلْغَيْبَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ أَيَّانَ يُبْعَثُونَ (٦٥)
Say, ‘No one in the heavens or the earth knows the Unseen except Allah, and they are not aware when they will be resurrected.’ (65)
بَلِ ٱدَّٰرَكَ عِلْمُهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ بَلْ هُمْ فِى شَكٍّ مِّنْهَا ۖ بَلْ هُم مِّنْهَا عَمُونَ (٦٦)
Do they comprehend the knowledge of the Hereafter? No, they are in doubt about it. Indeed, they are blind to it. (66)
وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَءِذَا كُنَّا تُرَٰبًا وَءَابَآؤُنَآ أَئِنَّا لَمُخْرَجُونَ (٦۷)
The faithless say, ‘What! When we and our fathers have become dust shall we be raised [from the dead]? (67)
لَقَدْ وُعِدْنَا هَـٰذَا نَحْنُ وَءَابَآؤُنَا مِن قَبْلُ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (٦۸)
We and our fathers were promised this before. [But] these are just myths of the ancients.’ (68)

Key Points

The Unseen (Ghayb) is of several types: one type Allah teaches to the Prophets, «تلك من انباء الغیب نوحیها الیك» (76), but another type, such as the knowledge of the time of the Hour (Qiyamah), is exclusive to His Holy Essence. The word «مَن» (man, meaning 'who') is used for conscious beings. Therefore, it is possible that in the heavens, besides the angels, there are other conscious beings who will be resurrected on the Day of Judgment just like humans. The phrase «بل ادّارك علمهم فى الآخرة» is similar to the phrase «كلاّسیعلمون ثمّ كلاّسیعلمون» (77). The word «ادّارك» (iddāraka) comes from tadāruk, meaning the joining and connecting of parts. This means that the polytheists suffer from intellectual fragmentation in this world, but in the Hereafter, their information will connect and they will attain knowledge—though what good will that do then! The word «اساطیر» (asāṭīr) is the plural of usṭūrah, meaning legend or fable. The disbelievers used to accuse the Prophets that what they said about the Day of Judgment were baseless matters stated merely for entertainment.

76) Hud, 49. 77) An-Naba, 4 - 5.

Messages

1- Denying the Resurrection is disbelief (kufr). «قال الّذین كفروا»

2- If we do not accept a truth, it does not mean that it will not happen or does not exist. «ءاذا كنّا»

3- Repeating a question, coupled with disbelief, is a sign of denial. «ءاذا - ءانّا»

4- One of the actions of the enemies is creating doubt in matters of belief. «ءانّا لمجرمون»

5- The antiquity of a statement is not a sign of its weakness. «لقد وعدنا هذا ... من قبل»

6- All Prophets promised the Resurrection. «لقد وعدنا هذا نحن و اباؤنا من قبل»

7- The transgressor seeks to find partners in crime. «نحن و اباؤنا»

8- Calling the words of the Prophets fables is one of the weapons of the disbelievers. «اساطیر الاوّلین»

Ayahs

قُلْ سِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (٦۹)
Say, ‘Travel over the land and observe how was the fate of the guilty.’ (69)
وَلَا تَحْزَنْ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا تَكُن فِى ضَيْقٍ مِّمَّا يَمْكُرُونَ (۷٠)
Do not grieve for them, and do not be upset by their guile. (70)

Key Points

The Quran has repeatedly encouraged people to travel through the earth using the phrases "Sīrū" (Travel) or "Afalam yasīrū" (Have they not traveled?), and in most cases, this encouragement has been for the purpose of warning, cautioning, and taking lessons from the lives of the criminals.

Messages

1- Visiting the remnants left behind by the oppressors of history is one of the tools for growth and education. «سیروا... فانظروا»

2- Human history has principles, laws, and traditions (Sunan), based on which the lessons of the past can serve as instruction for the present. «سیروا ... فانظروا»

3- Journeys must be purposeful. «سیروا... فانظروا»

4- Preserving the relics of the past is necessary for the admonition of future generations. «فانظروا»

5- Regions that have been destroyed by the wrath of Allah are national areas, and visiting them must be unimpeded for everyone. «سیروا... فانظروا»

6- Criminals have a bad outcome. «عاقبة المجرمین» (Continuous crime leads to disbelief and denial.)

7- Sin has worldly and otherworldly (Akhira) punishment. (The sample is indicative of the whole; observe the worldly wrath to realize the otherworldly wrath.) «عاقبة المجرمین»

8- When the outcome for criminals is destruction, why should we be saddened by their obstinacy and enmity? «لاتحزن»

9- The Prophet was compassionate even regarding the guidance of cunning enemies. «لاتحزن علیهم... یمكرون»

10- Prophets are under Divine protection. «لا تحزن - لا تكن» Allah consoles His Prophet.

Do not grieve, for I am your Comforter, And I am your Protector from all evils. If strangers turn their faces away from you, I am your Helper in this world and the next.

11- The capacity and endurance of human beings, even Prophets, are limited. «فى ضیق» The concern of the Prophet of Islam was that the deceit of the disbelievers might prove effective and His own propagation might remain ineffective, so Allah grants Him assurance and consolation. «لا تكن فى ضیق»

12- The enemies of Islam are constantly engaged in deceit and conspiracy. «یمكرون»

Ayahs

وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَـٰذَا ٱلْوَعْدُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۷۱)
They say, ‘When will this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful?’ (71)
قُلْ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَكُونَ رَدِفَ لَكُم بَعْضُ ٱلَّذِى تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (۷۲)
Say, ‘Perhaps there is right behind you some of what you seek to hasten.’ (72)
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ (۷۳)
Indeed your Lord is gracious to mankind, but most of them do not give thanks. (73)
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَيَعْلَمُ مَا تُكِنُّ صُدُورُهُمْ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ (۷٤)
Your Lord knows whatever their breasts conceal, and whatever they disclose. (74)

Key Points

When the prophets warned people about the Hour (Qiyamah) or the wrath of Allah, people demanded the time of its occurrence. However, since no one knows the time of the Hour except Allah, the prophets did not answer, and they only warned the people about the principle of Divine wrath, not its timing.

What is hidden in the breasts: «ما تكنّ صدورهم» (what they conceal in their breasts) is mentioned before the public deeds: «ما یعلنون» (what they declare). This may be for several reasons: A: Inner thoughts and intentions are the motivation for external actions, and thus they are prioritized (mentioned first). B: Knowing the inner reality (bāṭin) of individuals is more important than knowing their outward appearance (ẓāhir). C: Allah’s knowledge of hidden goals and thoughts poses a greater threat to those who deny the Resurrection (Ma'ad).

Messages

1- The disbelievers rush to witness the punishment with mockery. «متى هذا الوعد ان كنتم صادقین»

2- Not knowing the details of a reality is not a reason to deny its principle. (If we do not know the time of the Hour's occurrence, we should not deny its principle.) «متى هذا الوعد»

3- The root of the question about the time of the Hour's occurrence is doubt in prophethood. «ان كنتم صادقین»

4- It is not necessary to answer every question. (The disbelievers asked the Prophet to specify the time of the punishment, but he did not specify it.) «عسى أن یكون ردف لكم»

5- People's haste for the descent of punishment upon the disbelievers does not change Allah's wisdom. «عسى أن یكون... الّذى تستعجلون»

6- The world is not the place for complete retribution. «بعض الّذى تَستعجلون»

7- Divine punishments have a system, timing, and are gradual (stage by stage). «ردف لكم»

8- Fear and hope, side by side, are constructive. «ردف لكم... اِنّ ربّك لذو فضل»

9- The pursuit of Divine wrath against the disbelievers is not inevitable; they can be saved through repentance and changing their stance. «عسى...» ("عسى" [perhaps] means the path of return is open.)

10- Tasting a portion of worldly punishment can be a means of awareness, repentance, and a type of Divine grace. «ردف لكم بعض الّذى تستعجلون و اِنّ ربّك لذو فضل»

11- Delaying punishment is one of the methods of nurturing (tarbiyah), a sign of Divine kindness, and an opportunity for repentance. «اِنّ ربّك لذو فضل»

12- The ungrateful majority does not change the course of Allah's grace. «لذوفضل... اكثرهم لایشكرون»

13- Do not doubt Allah's knowledge. «اِنّ ربّك لیعلم»

14- When Allah knows everything, why commit sin and hypocrisy? «لیعلم ما تكنّ صدورهم و مایعلنون» Whether matters are hidden or public does not affect Allah's knowledge.

15- The Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah necessitates His complete awareness. «ربّك لیعلم»

16- Allah's knowledge of the hidden and the manifest guarantees the fulfillment of His promises, so do not hasten. «متى هذا الوعد... تستعجلون... انّ ربّك لیعلم...»

Ayah

وَمَا مِنْ غَآئِبَةٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مُّبِينٍ (۷٥)
There is no invisible thing in the heaven and the earth but it is in a manifest Book. (75)

Key Points

The letter (tā') in the word «غائبة» (ghā'ibah) is either for emphasis (mubālaghah), similar to «علاّمة» ('allāmah), meaning things that are very secret and hidden, or it is for femininity (ta'nīth) on the basis that the word «غائبة» is an adjective for the words «اشیاء» (ashyā') or «خصال» (khiṣāl), which are omitted (78). The unseen (ghayb) of the heavens and the earth includes all the hidden deeds of mankind, intentions, the occurrence of the Resurrection (Qiyāmah), the time of the descent of mercy or punishment, and other secrets. Perhaps the intent of «كتاب مبین» (Kitāb Mubīn - Clear Book) is the Preserved Tablet (Lawḥ Maḥfūẓ) and the endless knowledge of Allah.

78) Tafsīr Namūneh.

Messages

1- How numerous are the matters that human senses are incapable of perceiving. «غائبة فى السماء والارض»

2- The entire existence is under the supervision of Allah and is accounted for. «وما من غائبة... الاّ فى كتاب مبین»

Ayah

إِنَّ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ يَقُصُّ عَلَىٰ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ أَكْثَرَ ٱلَّذِى هُمْ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ (۷٦)
This Quran recounts for the Children of Israel most of what they differ about, (76)

Key Points

This verse indicates that the Torah and the Gospel, during the era of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, were unable to resolve the disputes of the People of the Book, and only the Quran, which is the sovereign over previous heavenly scriptures, possesses the power to resolve disputes.

Messages

1- The resolution of deep-rooted intellectual disputes, especially at the hands of a Prophet who was unlettered and did not attend school, is a sign of the miraculous nature and truthfulness of the Quran. «اِنّ هذا القرآن ...»

My beloved, who neither went to school nor wrote a line, By a mere glance taught the issues to a hundred teachers.

2- One of the blessings of the Quran is the resolution of disputes. «ان هذا القرآن ... یختلفون»

3- Disagreement originates from the people; otherwise, the divine schools of thought do not disagree. «هم فیه مختلفون»

Ayahs

وَإِنَّهُۥ لَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۷۷)
and it is indeed a guidance and mercy for the faithful. (77)
إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَقْضِى بَيْنَهُم بِحُكْمِهِۦ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۷۸)
Your Lord will decide between them by His judgement, and He is the All-mighty, the All-knowing. (78)
فَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَى ٱلْحَقِّ ٱلْمُبِينِ (۷۹)
So put your trust in Allah, for you indeed stand on the manifest truth. (79)

Messages

1- Resolving conflict is a clear example of Divine guidance and mercy. «لهدىً و رحمة... انّ ربّك یقضى»

2- Faith is the cause for receiving Divine mercy. «رحمة للمؤمنین»

3- Allah consoles His Prophet. «انّ ربّك یقضى»

4- Judgment is among the prerogatives of Divine Lordship. «انّ ربّك یقضى»

5- Divine decrees are exclusive to His Holy Essence, because ignorance, fear, instincts, and events do not affect Him, and they are one hundred percent just. «بحكمه»

6- In judgment, besides justice, knowledge and power are also prerequisites. «یقضى بینهم بحكمه و هوالعزیز العلیم»

7- Reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul) is one of the conditions for the success of a leader. «فتوكّل على اللّه»

8- Paying attention to the truthfulness of the path and affirming it safeguards a person against fleeting events. «انّك على الحقّ المبین»

9- The opponents of the Prophet were making excuses; otherwise, his path had no ambiguity. «انّك على الحقّ المبین»

10- Reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul) has meaning and bears fruit only on the path of truth, not on the path of falsehood. «فتوكّل على اللّه انّك على الحقّ المبین»

11- The leader must have faith in the truthfulness of his path. «انّك على الحقّ المبین»

Ayahs

إِنَّكَ لَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ وَلَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ ٱلدُّعَآءَ إِذَا وَلَّوْا۟ مُدْبِرِينَ (۸٠)
You cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf listen to your call when they turn their backs, (80)
وَمَآ أَنتَ بِهَـٰدِى ٱلْعُمْىِ عَن ضَلَـٰلَتِهِمْ ۖ إِن تُسْمِعُ إِلَّا مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَهُم مُّسْلِمُونَ (۸۱)
nor can you lead the blind out of their error. You can make only those hear you who believe in Our signs and have submitted. (81)

Key Points

Death and life in the culture of the Quran refer both to natural and material death and life, and to spiritual death and life. The Quran considers those who are not affected by the word of truth to be dead: «انّك لا تسمع الموتى» (Indeed, you cannot make the dead hear). Just as it repeatedly states: Do not consider the martyrs dead; they are alive and joyful, giving glad tidings to one another and enjoying divine sustenance. Therefore, the hard-hearted and stubborn living are dead, and the martyrs who have departed this world are alive.

It is better to elaborate and clarify the issue slightly:

Stages of Life

Life has several stages:

  1. Vegetative life. The Quran states: «یحیى الارض بعد موتها»(79) Allah revives the earth after its death by means of rain.
  2. Animal life. The Quran states: «یحییكم»(80) We gave you life.
  3. Spiritual life. «لینذر مَن كان حیّاً»(81) So that you may warn those who are alive. Meaning, those who possess sound intellect and nature (Fitrah). And He also states: The call of the prophets is for your life, «دعاكم لمایحییكم»(82).
  4. Political and social life. «ولكم فى القصاص حیاة»(83) If you establish justice by executing the ruling of Qisas (retaliation), the society is alive (otherwise, you have a dead society).
  5. Hereafterly life. «یالیتنى قدّمت لحیاتى»(84) O, would that I had sent forth [good deeds] for my [true] life in the Hereafter and accumulated a reserve.

Response to an Objection

We read in Tafsir Namuneh (85): The Wahhabi sect uses the Ayah «انّك لاتسمع الموتى» as a pretext for their deviant ideology, saying: The Noble Prophet has passed away and hears no speech; therefore, it makes no sense for us to visit the Prophet and address him with requests.

The response to the aforementioned sect is that the Ayah is presented as a general analogy; similar to comparing the hearts of the hard-hearted to stones in their imperviousness, «قلوبكم ... كالحجارة»(86), not that their hearts are like stones in every respect. This is because the Quran accepts the life of Barzakh (intermediate realm) concerning the martyrs, and narrations regarding this have been cited from both Shia and Sunni sources:

  1. Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab states in the book Al-Hadiyyah al-Saniyyah, p. 41: The Prophet, after his demise, possesses a life in Barzakh superior to the life of the martyrs, and He hears the greetings of those who greet Him.
  2. Numerous narrations have appeared in the books of the Shia and Ahl al-Sunnah regarding this, stating that the Prophet and the Imams علیهم السلام hear and respond to the speech of those who greet them from near and far, and even the deeds of the people are presented to them (87).
  3. We ourselves witness thousands of examples of Tawassul (seeking intercession) where the exact words and requests of the supplicants have been realized.
  4. We read in Sahih al-Bukhari: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله spoke with the disbelievers killed in the Battle of Badr, and when questioned by Umar, He said: «والّذى نفس محّمد بیده ما انتم باسمَع» By Him in Whose Hand is the soul of Muhammad, you do not hear better than them (88).
  5. At the end of the Battle of Jamal, Hadrat Ali علیه السلام ordered the corpse of Ka'b ibn Sur to be seated. Then He addressed him, who was destroyed, saying: Woe upon you! Knowledge and learning did not benefit you, and Satan led you astray and sent you to the Fire (89).

79) Ankabut, 50. 80) Jathiyah, 26. 81) Yasin, 70. 82) Anfal, 24. 83) Baqarah, 179. 84) Fajr, 24. 85) Tafsir Namuneh, Vol. 15, p. 543. 86) Baqarah, 74. 87) Kashf al-Irtiyab, p. 109. 88) Sahih al-Bukhari, Vol. 5, p. 97. 89) Sharh Nahj al-Balaghah by Ibn Abi al-Hadid, Vol. 1, p. 248.

Messages

  1. Entrust the matter of the blind-hearted to Allah. «فتوكّل... انّك لا تسمع الموتى»
  2. If the listener is hard-hearted, the word of truth, even from a pure and worthy speaker, will have no effect on him. Indeed, a burnt-out lamp will not light up when connected to any electricity. «انّك على الحقّ المبین - انّك لا تسمع الموتى»

What good is preaching to a dark heart? An iron nail does not penetrate stone.

  1. The ineffectiveness of the prophets' propagation is not due to ambiguity in the mission, but due to the blindness of the deniers' hearts. «اِنّك على الحقّ المبین - انّك لا تسمع الموتى»
  2. Faith is like the soul in the body. Those who do not believe, even after seeing miracles and hearing logic, are soulless corpses and dead. «انّك لا تسمع الموتى» Yes, hearing and accepting the truth is a sign of spiritual health.
  3. If a person reaches certainty, the rejection of the people will not affect him. «انك على الحق المبین - ولّوا مدبرین»
  4. Deaf people can understand something through gestures, but hearing people who have turned their backs on the truth and distanced themselves from the people of truth do not even understand gestures. «ولّوا مدبرین»
  5. Worse than rejection is the persistence of rejection. «ولّوا مدبرین»

Ayah

۞ وَإِذَا وَقَعَ ٱلْقَوْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ أَخْرَجْنَا لَهُمْ دَآبَّةً مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ تُكَلِّمُهُمْ أَنَّ ٱلنَّاسَ كَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا لَا يُوقِنُونَ (۸۲)
When the word [of judgement] falls upon them, We shall bring out for them an Animal from the earth who shall tell them that the people had no faith in Our signs. (82)

Key Points

This verse apparently relates to the event before the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah), where, whenever Allah's wrath becomes certain upon the people, the Almighty God, by His power, brings forth a living creature from the earth to speak to the people, and its speech is this: that the people do not believe.

Although the word dābbah is used for non-humans, this word has been used many times in the Qur'an in places that also include humans. And since the dābbah must be in a position where its word serves as a definitive proof against all disbelievers, it must be said that the intent of dābbah is an important personality who, in the narrations, has been interpreted as ‘Alī ibn Abī Ṭālib علیهما السلام.

Perhaps every one of Allah's Awliyā' (saints/friends) who undertakes an extraordinary uprising and movement in the End Times, and separates truth from falsehood, and the believer from the disbeliever, may also be included in this verse. «واللّه العالم»

Who and What is the Dābbah?

There are indications in the verse showing that this dābbah is a very important being, because:

  1. It appears on the verge of Divine wrath. «اذا وقع القول علیهم» Important statements necessitate being issued from important sources.

  2. It appears in an exceptional manner. «اخرجنا... من الارض»

  3. It speaks, «تكلّمهم», judges, and informs about the future of the people. «انّ الناس كانوا...»

  4. Its word, like the Trumpet of Isrāfīl, is the final word. «اذا وقع القول»

  5. The word dābbah, with tanwīn (nunation), is a sign of the greatness and magnitude of this being.

  6. The next verse relates to the Raj'ah (Return), where Allah's Awliyā' are brought back to life before the Day of Resurrection.

  7. The verse does not say: «بآیات اللّه» but says: «بآیاتنا». It is as if His signs are the very signs of Allah.

Synthesizing these indications found in the apparent meaning of the verse, along with the numerous narrations cited in the commentaries, leads us to the reality that we must say: The intent of «دابّة من الارض» is Hadrat ‘Alī علیه السلام. It is He who is worthy of speaking the final word, of stamping the forehead of some with the mark of non-belief, and of informing about the ominous future of the misguided.

Two Questions:

  1. Is the word dābbah not exclusive to non-human moving creatures?

Answer: No. The word dābbah has been used many times in the Qur'an to include humans, such as «و ما من دابّة فى الارض الاّ على اللّه رزقها»(90)

  1. Is applying the word 'moving creature' (junbandah) to Hadrat ‘Alī علیه السلام not an insult to him?

Answer: Never; just as applying the word bashar (mortal/human) to the Prophet is not an insult, and applying the words shay’ (thing), ‘ālim (knower), nūr (light), and wujūd (existence) to Allah is permissible, yet there is a difference between the Knowledge, Light, and Existence of Allah and that of others. (90) Hūd, 6.

Ayahs

وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ مِن كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ فَوْجًا مِّمَّن يُكَذِّبُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ (۸۳)
On that day We shall resurrect from every nation a group of those who denied Our signs, and they will be held in check. (83)
حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءُو قَالَ أَكَذَّبْتُم بِـَٔايَـٰتِى وَلَمْ تُحِيطُوا۟ بِهَا عِلْمًا أَمَّاذَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۸٤)
When they come, He will say, ‘Did you deny My signs without comprehending them in knowledge? What was it that you used to do?’ (84)

Key Points

Considering that all people will be resurrected on the Day of Judgment, "و حشرناهم فلم نغادر منهم احداً" (91), and the verse under discussion (83 of An-Naml) states that We shall gather only a group from every nation, it becomes clear that this verse does not relate to the Day of Judgment, but rather to the Raj'ah (Return), where a group will be brought back to life before the Day of Judgment. Examples of being brought back to life in this world have been mentioned in the Qur'an. For instance, in Surah Al-Baqarah, we read: Allah commanded a group to die, then He brought them back to life; "فقال لهم اللّه موتوا ثمّ احیاهم" (92). Or in another place, He says: We resurrected you in this very world after your death; "بَعَثناكم مِن بَعد مَوتِكم" (93). And He says to Prophet Jesus, عليه السلام: You bring forth the dead from the graves by My permission; "اذ تخرج المَوتى بِاِذنى" (94). And concerning one of Allah's saints, He says: We caused him to die for a hundred years, and after that period, We brought him back to life; "فاَماتَه اللّه مائة عام ثمّ بَعَثه" (95). Likewise, it has been narrated in many traditions (Riwāyāt) that before the Day of Judgment, individuals who were prominent in faith or disbelief will be brought back to life by the will of Allah, and they will live for a period under the rule of the people of truth, and the leaders of disbelief will be punished.

91) Al-Kahf, 47. 92) Al-Baqarah, 243. 93) Al-Baqarah, 56. 94) Al-Ma'idah, 110. 95) Al-Baqarah, 259.

Messages

  1. The general gathering of people is on the Day of Judgment, but the gathering of a specific group of disbelievers is in this very world. "و یوم نحشر من كلّ امّة فوجاً"

  2. The conflict between faith and disbelief has existed in all nations. "من كلّ امّة فوجاً ممّن یكذّب"

  3. The gathering before the Day of Judgment is specific to the absolute disbeliever. "ممّن یكذّب بایاتنا" (It is also mentioned in narrations: the Raj'ah is specific to the first-rank believers and disbelievers.)

  4. Do not deny something without knowledge. "أكذّبتم بایاتى ولم تحیطوا بها علما" (Beliefs must be based on definitive proof.)

  5. The actions of humans are a reflection of their beliefs and thoughts. (First denial, then wrongful deeds). "أكذّبتم - امّا ذا كنتم تعملون"

  6. Specific disbelievers, after being brought back to life in this world, remain under control until their trial begins. "یوزعون حتّى اذا جاؤا"

  7. During the period of Raj'ah (being brought back to life in this world), disbelievers will be reproached both regarding their beliefs and their conduct. "أكذّبتم - امّا ذا كنتم تعملون"

  8. Everyone must be accountable in the presence of Allah. "أكذّبتم بایاتى - امّا ذاكنتم تعملون"

Ayahs

وَوَقَعَ ٱلْقَوْلُ عَلَيْهِم بِمَا ظَلَمُوا۟ فَهُمْ لَا يَنطِقُونَ (۸٥)
And the word [of judgement] shall fall upon them for their wrongdoing, and they will not speak. (85)
أَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّا جَعَلْنَا ٱلَّيْلَ لِيَسْكُنُوا۟ فِيهِ وَٱلنَّهَارَ مُبْصِرًا ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (۸٦)
Do they not see that We made the night that they may rest in it, and the day to provide visibility. There are indeed signs in that for a people who have faith. (86)

Key Points

The role of the darkness of night in calming the nerves and the brightness of day in human activity is one of the scientifically proven issues of today. (96) The presentation of the issue of sleep and wakefulness after the issue of the Return (Raj'ah) and the Resurrection (Ma'ad) perhaps carries the message that you should not be surprised by the Return and the Resurrection, because these two are like sleep and wakefulness. Night is an example of Allah's mercy upon people and a sign of Divine wisdom and power. Ignoring the role of the night is an example of the ignorant denial that was discussed in the previous Ayah.

Means of Tranquility in the Quran Some matters have been introduced in the Quran as means of tranquility (Sakinah), including: 1. Remembrance of Allah. «الا بذكر اللّه تَطمئنّ القلوب» (97) 2. Divine aid. «هوالّذى أنزل السّكينة فى قلوب المؤمنین» (98) 3. Sacred relics and objects. «أن یأتیكم التّابوت فیه سكينة من ربّكم» (99) 4. Encouragement by the Divine Guardians (Awliya). «اِنّ صلوتك سَكنٌ لهم» (100) 5. Shelter and home. «واللّه جَعَل لكم من بیوتكم سَكنا» (101) 6. Spouse. «خَلَق لكم من انفسكم ازواجاً لتسكنوا» (102) 7. Night. «انّا جعلنا الّیل لیسكنوا» (103)

It is worth noting that the revelation of the Quran, the ascension (Mi'raj) of the Prophet, and the time for supplication and intimate prayer (Munajat) occur during the night; although some deviants misuse the blessing of the night and its darkness and commit sins.

  • The present age seeks tranquility elsewhere and has therefore not achieved results. Today, the world relies on technology, weapons, wealth, power, and diplomatic relations, and due to the loss of spirituality, it does not see the color of peace. Many individuals are addicted to narcotics, wine, lust, and so on, and are drowned in sin; to grasp the reality, it is sufficient to refer to the statistics of crimes worldwide.

Messages

1- The punishment of the oppressors is certain. «وقع»

2- The promise of Divine punishments has been previously told to the oppressors. «القول»

3- The cause of human misery is the human himself. «بماظلموا»

4- Denying and rebelling against the serious warnings of the prophets is oppression against oneself. «بماظلموا»

5- On the Day of Judgment, the oppressor has nothing to say. «لاینطقون»

6- Those who do not take a lesson from the coming and going of night and day are subject to criticism. Most of the Quran's criticisms are directed at those who live heedless lives. «ألم یروا»

7- The system of creation is harmonious with human needs, and this harmony is a sign of the existence of a Wise and Unique Planner. «لیسكنوا»

8- In your life plan, dedicate the night specifically for rest. «لیسكنوا فیه»

9- The role of faith and religious contemplation is that it views existence as meaningful, purposeful, and a sign of power and wisdom. «لیسكنوا - مبصراً - لآیات»

10- Rest must be a prelude to work and effort. «لیسكنوا - مبصراً» The brightness of the day is for work, just as Ayah 12 of Surah Al-Isra' states: «مبصرة لتبتغوا فضلاً» so that you may seek the bounty of your Lord (and engage in effort and work).

11- Only the people of faith take lessons from the Divine signs. «انّ فى ذلك لآیات لقوم یؤمنون»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَفَزِعَ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا مَن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَكُلٌّ أَتَوْهُ دَٰخِرِينَ (۸۷)
The day when the trumpet is blown, whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified, except such as Allah wishes, and all will come to Him in utter humility. (87)

Key Points

The word dakhūr (داخر) means humiliation and smallness (lowliness). The Qur'an speaks of two "blasts of the Trumpet" (nafkh al-ṣūr): one blast that causes the death of everyone, and another blast that causes life and presence (resurrection). In Ayah 68 of Surah Az-Zumar, we read: «و نُفِخَ فى الصُّور فَصَعِقَ مَن فى السموات و مَن فِى الارض الاّ مَن شاءاللّه ثمّ نُفِخ فیه اُخرى فاذا هم قیامٌ یَنظُرون» The Trumpet of "Isrāfīl" will be blown, and all creatures in the heavens and the earth will die, except those whom Allah wills. Then it will be blown into again, and suddenly all creation will rise up and be in expectation (of accountability and recompense). The intent of the blast in this Ayah is either the first blast, due to the word fazaʿ (terror), or the second blast, due to [the word] ātūh (they will come to Him), or both blasts, due to both words. «واللّه العالم» (And Allah knows best). In narrations, we read that the angel responsible for blowing the Trumpet is named Isrāfīl. In the supplication of Umm Dāwūd, we read: «الّلهم صلّ على اسرافیل حامل عرشك و صاحب الصّور» (104) The group that is safe from the terror and dread at the start of the Resurrection, referred to as «الاّ مَن شاءاللّه» (except those whom Allah wills), are introduced two verses later: «مَن جاء بالحسنة فله خیرٌ منها وهم من فَزَع یومئذٍ آمنون» (Whoever comes with a good deed will have better than it, and they will be safe from the terror of that Day). Just as we read elsewhere: «لایَحزنهم الفَزَع الاكبر» (105) The greatest terror of that Day will not grieve them.

104) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn. 105) Al-Anbiyā', 103.

Messages

1- The collapse of the cosmic order, the death of humans, and the start of the Resurrection occur through the creation of a terrifying sound (the blowing of the Trumpet). «ینفخ فى الصور»

2- There are living and accountable beings in the heavens, and these beings will also undergo gathering (resurrection) and the Day of Judgment. «من فى السموات - كل أتوه داخرین»

3- On the Day of Resurrection, everyone will feel small (lowly) internally. «داخرین»

Ayah

وَتَرَى ٱلْجِبَالَ تَحْسَبُهَا جَامِدَةً وَهِىَ تَمُرُّ مَرَّ ٱلسَّحَابِ ۚ صُنْعَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَتْقَنَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَفْعَلُونَ (۸۸)
You see the mountains, which you suppose, to be stationary, while they drift like passing clouds—the handiwork of Allah who has made everything faultless. He is indeed well aware of what you do. (88)

Key Points

The word ṣun‘ (صُنع), derived from ṣan‘at (industry/craft), refers to an action based on knowledge, precision, and skill. The first individuals who discovered the movement of the Earth were the Italian Galileo and the Polish Copernicus, who lived in the late 16th and early 17th centuries AD. Galileo, under pressure from the Church, outwardly recanted his statement. However, the Quran informed about the movement of the Earth in many verses a thousand years before Galileo. (106) Of course, some commentators consider the movement of the mountains to be an allusion to their disintegration after the blowing of the Trumpet (Nafkhat al-Ṣūr), which was mentioned in the previous verse, and based on this meaning, they have derived other messages from the noble verse. (107)

106) Tafsir Namūneh. 107) Tafsir Rāhnamā.

Messages

1- The universe is in motion. Even mountains, which appear stationary, are moving. «تحسبها جامدة و هى تمرّ»

2- Informing about the movement of the mountains is among the scientific miracles of the Quran. «تمّر مرّالسحاب»

3- The movement of the mountains is inseparable from the movement of the Earth; therefore, the Earth also moves. «تمّر»

4- The movement of the mountains is swift, like the movement of the clouds. «مرّالسّحاب» And the stability of the Earth, despite its movement, is among the Divine signs.

5- The movement of the mountains is a sign of Allah’s wise power. «صُنع اللّه»

6- In the Divine perspective, everything is firm and secure in its proper place. «اَتقن كلّ شى ء»

Ayahs

مَن جَآءَ بِٱلْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُۥ خَيْرٌ مِّنْهَا وَهُم مِّن فَزَعٍ يَوْمَئِذٍ ءَامِنُونَ (۸۹)
Whoever brings virtue shall receive [a reward] better than it; and on that day they will be secure from terror. (89)
وَمَن جَآءَ بِٱلسَّيِّئَةِ فَكُبَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ فِى ٱلنَّارِ هَلْ تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۹٠)
But whoever brings vice—they shall be cast on their faces into the Fire [and told:] ‘Shall you not be requited for what you used to do?’ (90)

Key Points

The word "كُبّت" (kubbat) means to be thrown down headlong (or overthrown). The pleasures, transgressions, and excesses that arise from sin in this world will be transformed into humiliation and disgrace in the Hereafter by burning in the Fire. One of the blessings of the Quran is that it has opened the path to growth and felicity for everyone, stating: whoever performs a good deed will be granted a reward better than it, without age, race, or gender being a factor. Goodness is also stated absolutely as "الحسنة" (al-hasanah) so that it encompasses all good deeds, such as accepting true leadership, the true path, true speech, a true profession, a true choice, and so on... some of which have been mentioned in narrations. However, any good deed, from whomever it may come, if it is not destroyed by hypocrisy (riya'), arrogance, vanity ('ujb), or sin, and reaches its destination soundly, will receive a doubled reward. "مَن جاء بالحسنة" (Whoever comes with a good deed). We read in the two preceding verses that after the blowing of the Trumpet, everyone will be terrified, except those whom Allah wills. In these verses, He states: Allah grants security from terror and fright to those who bring goodness with them to the scene of the Resurrection.

Messages

1- Allah's knowledge of our good deeds is a factor encouraging us to perform good deeds. "انّه خبیر بما تفعلون مَن جاء بالحسنة"

2- More important than performing a good deed in this world is bringing it to the scene of the Resurrection. "مَن جاء بالحسنة فله خیر منها"

3- The future depends on our deeds. "من جاء ... فله ..." (Every action has a reaction).

4- We should mention good deeds before evil deeds. "من جاءبالحسنة" (Whoever comes with a good deed), followed by "من جاءبالسیئة" (Whoever comes with an evil deed).

5- Encouraging the righteous is necessary for the growth of goodness. "من جاء - فله خیر منها"

6- Encouragement and threat are effective when placed side by side. "من جاء بالحسنة - من جاء بالسیئة"

7- Divine rewards are superior to our deeds. (The reward for some deeds is double, "الضِعف" (108); for some, several times over, "اَضعافاً" (109); for some, ten times over, "فله عَشر" (110); up to seven hundred times and more, all of which are encompassed by "خیرٌ منها" (better than it)).

8- Good-doing is the factor for salvation on the Day of Resurrection. "مَن جاء بالحسنه - مِن فزع یومئذ آمنون"

9- The superiority of Divine rewards is absolute; they are eternal in terms of time and several times greater than our deeds in terms of quantity. "خیرٌ منها"

10- Worse than the punishment is the humiliation of man. "كُبَّت وجوههم"

11- Divine punishments are the reflection of our deeds and the embodiment of our actions. Indeed, those who turn their faces away from the truth after hearing it will fall into Hell on that Day, face first. "هل تجزون"

12- Divine rewards are manifold; "خیرٌ منها" (better than it), but His punishments are single and just. "ما كنتم تعملون"

13- The Resurrection is physical. "وجوههم" (their faces).

14- Man's deeds will be embodied on the Day of Resurrection and will afflict him. "هل تجزون الاّ ما كنتم تعملون"

Ayah

إِنَّمَآ أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ رَبَّ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْبَلْدَةِ ٱلَّذِى حَرَّمَهَا وَلَهُۥ كُلُّ شَىْءٍ ۖ وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ (۹۱)
[Say], ‘I have been commanded to worship the Lord of this city who has made it inviolable and to whom all things belong, and I have been commanded to be among those who submit [to Allah], (91)

Key Points

The day Makkah was conquered by the Muslims, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله entered the Ka'bah and broke the idols. He then stood at the threshold of the Ka'bah's door and said: «اِنّ اللّه قد حرّم مكّة» Allah has held Makkah sacred from the very first day until the Day of Resurrection. (111)

111) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- The Prophet is under divine command. «اُمرت - واُمرت»

2- The preacher must declare his firmness and decisiveness to the people and say: Whether you believe or not, I will continue my path. «اُمرتُ - واُمرتُ»

3- No one, under any circumstances, is needless of worship, not even the Prophet. «اُمرت أن أعبد»

4- Worship must be by divine command, not a human fabrication. «اُمرت أن أعبد»

5- Only those who are themselves pioneers have the right to invite people to worship. «اُمرت»

6- Servitude must be accompanied by heartfelt submission. «أعبد - من المسلمین»

7- The philosophy behind serving Allah is His Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and His ownership. «أعبد ربّ - وله كل شى ء»

8- Both perfect yourself and join those who are perfected. «أعبد - من المسلمین»

9- Everything is under His nurturing care. «ربّ هذه البلدة»

10- Makkah holds a special respect and status. «حرّمها»

Ayah

وَأَنْ أَتْلُوَا۟ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ ۖ فَمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ فَإِنَّمَا يَهْتَدِى لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن ضَلَّ فَقُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُنذِرِينَ (۹۲)
and to recite the Quran.’ Whoever is guided is guided only for his own good, and as for him who goes astray, say, ‘I am just one of the warners.’ (92)

Key Points

The beginning of this Surah was about the importance of the Quran; its end also discusses the Noble Quran. The Quran says regarding its own recitation: «و ما تكون فى شأن و ما تتلوا منه من قرآن و لاتعملون من عمل الاّ كنّا علیكم شهودا»(112) O Prophet! We are witnesses over three things: A: Over all your deeds. B: Over your recitation of the Quran. C: Over all the deeds that you people perform. Meaning, all the affairs and deeds of the Prophet and all the deeds of the people are placed on one side, and the recitation of the Quran by that noble personage is placed on the other side, and this is a sign of the immense importance of reciting the Quran.

112) Yunus, 61.

Messages

1- The one who brings the Quran must also be a person of recitation himself. «اتلوا القرآن»

2- The most important mission of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, after Monotheism (Tawhid), is the recitation of the Divine verses to the people. «اتلوا القرآن»

3- The axis of propagation must be the Quran. «أن اتلوا القرآن فمن اهتدى...»

4- The duty of the propagator is to convey the truths to the people; acceptance or non-acceptance relates to the people themselves. «ان اتلوا القرآن فمن اهتدى... و من ضلّ»

5 - The recitation of the Quran is the prelude to guidance. «اتلوا القرآن فمن اهتدى»

6- The benefit or harm of people's faith or disbelief returns to themselves. «لنفسه»

7- The Prophet is not responsible for those who go astray. «فقل انما انا من المنذرین»

Ayah

وَقُلِ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ سَيُرِيكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ فَتَعْرِفُونَهَا ۚ وَمَا رَبُّكَ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (۹۳)
And say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah. Soon He will show you His signs, and you will recognize them.’ Your Lord is not oblivious of what you do. (93)

Key Points

"الحمدللّه" (All praise belongs to Allah) is the best phrase for praising the Divine, which the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was commanded to say repeatedly, and which all Muslims must repeat every day in Surah Al-Hamd (Al-Fatiha).

Sa'di says regarding man's heedlessness of his ultimate end:

Now, O sleeper, you must be awake, What good is waking when death arrives? You are heedless, thinking of profit and wealth, While the capital of your life is being wasted. Strive now, while the water is only waist-high, Not when the flood has passed over your head. Alexander, who ruled the world, Left the world behind the moment he departed. It was not possible for him to ask the world to grant him a moment's respite.

Messages

1- The Divine mission (Prophethood) is a favor for which one must be grateful. «انّما اُمرت... و قل الحمدللّه»

2- What you have seen of the Divine signs so far is only a part of them; He will show you other signs in the future as well. «سیریكم آیاته»

3- Do not take the Divine respites as a sign of Allah's heedlessness. «و ما ربّك بغافل»

4- Allah's constant supervision is among the attributes of His Lordship (Rububiyyah). «وما ربّك بغافل»

5- Man is responsible for his own deeds. «وما ربّك بغافل عمّاتعملون»

6- Do not worry about the disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk) of the opponents. «و ما ربّك بغافل عمّاتعملون»
